content.text:
All six core agents are composed. Now presenting the full coordination architecture and master build plan.

---

## saralogy.com — Zero to One Coordination Architecture

### How This Works

I am the **Coordinator**. I own the master plan, route tasks to agents, synthesize their outputs, and escalate to you when a decision requires human authority. Every agent below is a real AI agent I can spawn — each with a distinct personality, voice, and specialty. You never talk to agents directly unless I surface a specific question. You talk to me.

---

### The Agent Roster

| # | Agent | Voice | Color | Role | Phase |
|---|-------|-------|-------|------|-------|
| **COORDINATOR** | **PAI (me)** | Rachel | — | Master plan, routing, synthesis, escalation | All |
| **A1** | Systems Architect | James 🇦🇺 | `#1ABC9C` | Astro structure, URL schema, content collections, subdomain plan | Phase 1 |
| **A2** | SEO Architect | Baron 🇬🇧 | `#009688` | Technical SEO spec, schema strategy, internal linking rules, CWV targets | Phase 1 |
| **A3** | DevOps Architect | James 🇦🇺 | `#1ABC9C` | Vercel pipeline, GitHub CI/CD, build config, environments | Phase 1 |
| **A4** | Content Strategist | Jeremy 🇺🇸 | `#8BC34A` | Pillar outlines, content calendar, page templates | Phase 2 |
| **A5** | AEO Specialist | Rachel 🇺🇸 | `#FFC107` | FAQ blocks, structured data copy, entity markup | Phase 2 |
| **A6** | Frontend Engineer | Jeremy 🇺🇸 | `#1ABC9C` | Astro components, layouts, TypeScript types | Phase 3 |
| **A7** | SEO Writer | *(spawned per page)* | — | Keyword-optimized content per page | Phase 3 |
| **A8** | Analytics Engineer | *(spawned at launch)* | — | GA4, Search Console, Vercel Analytics setup | Phase 4 |

---

### What Requires Your Authority (User Gates)

These are hard stops — nothing proceeds without you personally doing the action:

| Gate | Action Required | When |
|------|----------------|------|
| **G1** | Create GitHub repo `berksaraloglu/saralogy.com` (or confirm existing path) | Before Phase 1 agents launch |
| **G2** | Authenticate Vercel account and link GitHub repo | Before Phase 3 |
| **G3** | Configure DNS — point saralogy.com → Vercel | Before Phase 4 |
| **G4** | Approve final brand voice on the `/about` page | Phase 2 output review |
| **G5** | Verify factual accuracy on every `/career/*` page | Phase 3 output review |
| **G6** | Verify song info on every `/music/*` page | Phase 3 output review |
| **G7** | Approve analytics setup (GA4 tracking ID, GSC verification file) | Phase 4 |
| **G8** | Final launch decision — flip DNS live | Phase 4 |

I will never bypass a Gate. When a gate is hit, I stop and tell you exactly what to do.

---

### The Phased Build Plan

```
PHASE 1 — Architecture Committee (2–3 hours, PARALLEL)
  A1 Systems Architect → Astro project spec + URL schema + content collection types
  A2 SEO Architect     → Technical SEO spec + schema markup strategy + CWV targets
  A3 DevOps Architect  → Vercel config + GitHub Actions + environment plan
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ★ YOU: Review outputs, approve architecture decisions, create GitHub repo (G1)
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PHASE 2 — Content Architecture (2 hours, PARALLEL)
  A4 Content Strategist → 3 pillar outlines + content calendar + page templates
  A5 AEO Specialist     → FAQ blocks for /about, schema copy, entity page spec
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ★ YOU: Approve /about voice and career page accuracy (G4, G5)
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PHASE 3 — Build (8–10 hours, SEQUENTIAL)
  A6 Frontend Engineer → Astro scaffold + components + layouts + content templates
  A7 SEO Writer ×3     → /about, /career/siemens, /ai-marketing-dach (3 MVP pages)
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ★ YOU: Authenticate Vercel (G2), review each page draft
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PHASE 4 — Launch (2 hours)
  A8 Analytics Engineer → GA4 + Search Console + Vercel Analytics
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  ★ YOU: DNS config (G3), analytics approval (G7), launch decision (G8)
  ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

PHASE 5+ — Expand (ongoing, 2–3h/week)
  Spawn content agents per page type as needed
  SEO Writer for each new career/music/project page
  Analytics Agent for monthly performance review
```

---

### Agent Communication Protocol

```
Agent → Coordinator (me): returns structured output + flags for user
Coordinator → Agent: task brief with full context + ISC criteria
Coordinator → User (you): synthesized decision points only
User → Coordinator: approvals, corrections, personal content input
```

Agents don't know about each other. I know about all of them. I feed each agent exactly what it needs and no more. Agent outputs get synthesized before they reach you — you don't read raw agent output unless you want to.

---

### Right Now — What I Need From You (G1)

Before I launch the Architecture Committee, I need one answer:

**Where should the project live?**

Option A: `~/saralogy` on your local machine (I scaffold it here, you push to GitHub)
Option B: You create the GitHub repo first and give me the path after cloning

Once you confirm, I immediately launch A1, A2, and A3 in parallel. They'll each produce their spec documents. I synthesize, you approve the architecture decisions, and Phase 2 starts the same day.

---

**The Architecture Committee is ready. Agents are composed. Waiting on your go.**

input.content:
# saralogy.com — Project Brief

## Owner
Berk Saraloglu — Marketing leader, AI tool builder, songwriter, Turkish-German expat in Hamburg

## Central Thesis
AI-native marketing is the only viable path for DACH SMEs in the next five years — and most of them are years behind. This site proves it from two directions simultaneously: from inside (marketing leadership at enterprise scale: Siemens and others) and from outside (building the AI tools to make it accessible: vision-intelligence, resume-matcher).

## Target Audiences
1. **Recruiters** (Hamburg/DACH, Senior Marketing Manager roles, €85-95K)
2. **DACH SME founders** (searching for AI marketing expertise)
3. **Music collaborators / A&R** (indie songwriter audience)
4. **Collaborators / builders** (AI product people)

## Tech Stack (CONFIRMED)
- **Framework:** Astro 4+ (TypeScript, zero-JS default, content collections)
- **Deployment:** Vercel
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS
- **No CMS** — Astro content collections are the content layer

## Confirmed URL Structure
```
saralogy.com/                    → Hub / intent router
saralogy.com/about               → Entity page (LLM-optimized)
saralogy.com/work                → Availability signal + hire me
saralogy.com/now                 → Monthly update page
saralogy.com/career              → Career index
saralogy.com/career/[company]    → Living document tenure pages
saralogy.com/music               → Music hub
saralogy.com/music/[song-slug]   → Song archaeology pages
saralogy.com/music/process       → Songwriting process
saralogy.com/music/gear          → Gear page
saralogy.com/projects            → Projects index
saralogy.com/projects/[slug]     → Project pitch pages (non-technical)
saralogy.com/trips               → Trip index
saralogy.com/trips/[country]     → Country essays
saralogy.com/trips/[country]/[city] → City deep dives
saralogy.com/ai-marketing-dach   → Pillar page (Cluster 1)
saralogy.com/writing             → Essays index (The Intersection format)
saralogy.com/writing/[slug]      → Individual essays
```

## The Two-Layer Page Architecture
Every page has TWO layers:
- **Permanent layer:** Story, learnings, historical facts (never edited after publish)
- **Living layer:** "Current Relevance" section, links to current projects, "What I'd do differently" (updated annually)

## SEO Topical Clusters
1. **AI-Native Marketing for DACH SMEs** — pillar: /ai-marketing-dach
2. **Indie Songwriter Process** — pillar: /music/process
3. **Expat Career Navigation in Germany** — pillar: /career

## Content Production Constraint
≤3 hours/week at steady state (post-employment). Architecture must survive this.

## Known Projects to Feature
- vision-intelligence (AI marketing tool for SMEs)
- resume-matcher (ATS optimization)
- saralogy.com itself (meta)

## Known Career Tenures
- Siemens (enterprise B2B marketing)
- [others to be added by Berk]

## Key AEO Requirements
- Person schema on /about
- MusicRecording schema on song pages
- SoftwareApplication schema on project pages
- FAQPage schema on /about and pillar pages
- WorkExperience schema on career pages

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# saralogy.com — Project Brief

## Owner
Berk Saraloglu — Marketing leader, AI tool builder, songwriter, Turkish-German expat in Hamburg

## Central Thesis
AI-native marketing is the only viable path for DACH SMEs in the next five years — and most of them are years behind. This site proves it from two directions simultaneously: from inside (marketing leadership at enterprise scale: Siemens and others) and from outside (building the AI tools to make it accessible: vision-intelligence, resume-matcher).

## Target Audiences
1. **Recruiters** (Hamburg/DACH, Senior Marketing Manager roles, €85-95K)
2. **DACH SME founders** (searching for AI marketing expertise)
3. **Music collaborators / A&R** (indie songwriter audience)
4. **Collaborators / builders** (AI product people)

## Tech Stack (CONFIRMED)
- **Framework:** Astro 4+ (TypeScript, zero-JS default, content collections)
- **Deployment:** Vercel
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS
- **No CMS** — Astro content collections are the content layer

## Confirmed URL Structure
```
saralogy.com/                    → Hub / intent router
saralogy.com/about               → Entity page (LLM-optimized)
saralogy.com/work                → Availability signal + hire me
saralogy.com/now                 → Monthly update page
saralogy.com/career              → Career index
saralogy.com/career/[company]    → Living document tenure pages
saralogy.com/music               → Music hub
saralogy.com/music/[song-slug]   → Song archaeology pages
saralogy.com/music/process       → Songwriting process
saralogy.com/music/gear          → Gear page
saralogy.com/projects            → Projects index
saralogy.com/projects/[slug]     → Project pitch pages (non-technical)
saralogy.com/trips               → Trip index
saralogy.com/trips/[country]     → Country essays
saralogy.com/trips/[country]/[city] → City deep dives
saralogy.com/ai-marketing-dach   → Pillar page (Cluster 1)
saralogy.com/writing             → Essays index (The Intersection format)
saralogy.com/writing/[slug]      → Individual essays
```

## The Two-Layer Page Architecture
Every page has TWO layers:
- **Permanent layer:** Story, learnings, historical facts (never edited after publish)
- **Living layer:** "Current Relevance" section, links to current projects, "What I'd do differently" (updated annually)

## SEO Topical Clusters
1. **AI-Native Marketing for DACH SMEs** — pillar: /ai-marketing-dach
2. **Indie Songwriter Process** — pillar: /music/process
3. **Expat Career Navigation in Germany** — pillar: /career

## Content Production Constraint
≤3 hours/week at steady state (post-employment). Architecture must survive this.

## Known Projects to Feature
- vision-intelligence (AI marketing tool for SMEs)
- resume-matcher (ATS optimization)
- saralogy.com itself (meta)

## Known Career Tenures
- Siemens (enterprise B2B marketing)
- [others to be added by Berk]

## Key AEO Requirements
- Person schema on /about
- MusicRecording schema on song pages
- SoftwareApplication schema on project pages
- FAQPage schema on /about and pillar pages
- WorkExperience schema on career pages

input.prompt:
# Dynamic Agent: Technical Specialist Analytical Systematic (A1 — Systems Architect)
Voice: James | Color: #1ABC9C

You are A1, the Systems Architect on the saralogy.com Architecture Committee. You are methodical, precise, and thorough. You produce decisions, not recommendations — your output is the spec the frontend engineer will implement.

## Your Task

Produce the complete Astro project structure, URL architecture, content collection Zod schemas, and subdomain plan for saralogy.com.

Read the project brief first:
`/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/PROJECT_BRIEF.md`

## What to Produce

Write your full spec to `/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/specs/A1-systems-architecture.md`

Your spec must cover:

### 1. Astro Project Directory Structure
Full file tree showing every directory and key file. Include:
- `src/content/` collections (career, music, projects, trips, writing)
- `src/pages/` routing (static + dynamic routes)
- `src/components/` organization
- `src/layouts/` templates
- `src/styles/`
- `public/` static assets
- Config files: `astro.config.mjs`, `tsconfig.json`, `tailwind.config.mjs`

### 2. Content Collection Zod Schemas
Write the actual TypeScript/Zod schema for each collection type. These will go in `src/content/config.ts`. Be precise — every field, every type, every optional vs required decision.

Collections needed:
- `career` (tenure pages — two-layer: permanent + living sections)
- `music` (song archaeology pages)
- `projects` (non-technical pitch pages)
- `trips` (country + city essays)
- `writing` (essays — "The Intersection" format)

For `career` specifically: the schema must enforce the two-layer architecture (permanent fields: role, company, dates, story, wins, lessons + living fields: currentRelevance, projectsFromHere, lastReviewed).

### 3. Dynamic Route Plan
Map every URL pattern to its Astro file:
- `src/pages/career/[slug].astro` → which collection
- `src/pages/music/[slug].astro` → which collection
- etc.

### 4. Shared TypeScript Types
Any types that span collections (e.g., `ContentStatus`, `AudienceTag`, `SEOFields`).

### 5. Subdomain Plan
Decide which subdomains to reserve now (even if unused):
- `app.saralogy.com` — for AI service product
- `blog.saralogy.com` — possibly later
- Others?

State clearly: which are configured on Vercel now, which are deferred.

### 6. Architecture Decisions Log
For each non-obvious decision, write one line: Decision | Rationale | Alternative rejected.

## Output Format

Write a clean, implementation-ready markdown spec. Use code blocks for all TypeScript. Structure it so a developer can follow it top-to-bottom.

When done, voice your completion:
```bash
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:31337/notify -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"message\":\"A1 Systems Architect complete. Astro structure and schemas delivered.\",\"voice_id\":\"ZQe5CZNOzWyzPSCn5a3c\",\"title\":\"A1 Systems Architect\",\"voice_enabled\":true}"
```

input.prompt:
# Dynamic Agent: Research Specialist Skeptical Thorough (A2 — SEO Architect)
Voice: Baron | Color: #009688

You are A2, the SEO Architect on the saralogy.com Architecture Committee. You are evidence-driven, skeptical of generic SEO advice, and produce implementation-ready specs — not strategy decks.

## Your Task

Produce the complete technical SEO and AEO spec for saralogy.com.

Read the project brief first:
`/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/PROJECT_BRIEF.md`

## What to Produce

Write your full spec to `/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/specs/A2-seo-aeo-spec.md`

Your spec must cover:

### 1. Technical SEO Foundations
- `robots.txt` — exact content to ship
- `sitemap.xml` — strategy (static vs auto-generated by Astro, which pages included/excluded, update frequency)
- Canonical tag rules — when to set canonical, when to omit, how to handle paginated content
- `hreflang` — decision: German/English variants now or defer? State rationale.
- 404 handling — Astro/Vercel config

### 2. Core Web Vitals Targets
For each metric (LCP, FID/INP, CLS), state:
- Target threshold (Good / Needs Improvement / Poor boundaries)
- Astro-specific implementation rules to hit it (image optimization, font loading, JS deferral)
- The one thing that most commonly tanks each metric for Astro sites

### 3. Structured Data (Schema.org) — Complete Spec
For each page type, write the exact JSON-LD schema to implement. Be explicit about every field.

Page types:
- Homepage — `WebSite` + `Person`
- `/about` — `Person` (full: alumniOf, knowsAbout, hasOccupation, nationality, sameAs for social profiles)
- `/career/[slug]` — `Person` + `WorkExperience` (or `OrganizationRole`)
- `/music/[slug]` — `MusicRecording` (name, byArtist, duration, genre, description)
- `/projects/[slug]` — `SoftwareApplication` (name, applicationCategory, operatingSystem, offers)
- `/ai-marketing-dach` + `/music/process` + `/career` pillars — `Article` + `FAQPage`
- `/now` — `BlogPosting`

For `/about` specifically: include a `FAQPage` block answering "Who is Berk Saraloglu?", "What does Berk Saraloglu do?", "Where is Berk Saraloglu based?" — these are the exact queries LLMs receive.

### 4. Meta Tag Templates
For each content type, provide the exact meta tag template with variables:
- `<title>` — pattern and character limits
- `<meta name="description">` — pattern and character limits
- `<meta property="og:*">` — complete Open Graph set
- `<meta name="twitter:*">` — Twitter Card set

### 5. Internal Linking Rules
The topical authority triangle: career pages → /ai-marketing-dach pillar → project pages → back to career.
Write explicit rules:
- Every career page MUST link to: [list]
- Every project page MUST link to: [list]
- Every song page MUST link to: [list]
- Pillar pages MUST link to: [list]

### 6. Keyword Clusters — Implementation Map
For each of the 3 confirmed clusters:
1. AI-Native Marketing for DACH SMEs
2. Indie Songwriter Process
3. Expat Career Navigation in Germany

List:
- Primary keyword (page title target)
- 3-5 secondary keywords (body content targets)
- LSI terms (semantic variations)
- Which existing page targets this cluster

### 7. AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) Checklist
Specific implementation steps to maximize LLM citation probability:
- Entity disambiguation strategy
- How to structure the FAQ blocks (exact format)
- sameAs links to include on Person schema (LinkedIn, GitHub, Spotify, etc.)
- NAP consistency rules (Name/handle/location appear identically across all pages)

### 8. SEO Anti-patterns to Avoid
5 specific things that commonly hurt Astro personal sites on SEO. State each and the fix.

## Output Format

Implementation-ready spec. Every code example is complete and copy-paste ready. No hand-waving.

When done, voice your completion:
```bash
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:31337/notify -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"message\":\"A2 SEO Architect complete. Technical SEO and AEO spec delivered.\",\"voice_id\":\"onwK4e9ZLuTAKqWW03F9\",\"title\":\"A2 SEO Architect\",\"voice_enabled\":true}"
```

toolUseResult.prompt:
# Dynamic Agent: Research Specialist Skeptical Thorough (A2 — SEO Architect)
Voice: Baron | Color: #009688

You are A2, the SEO Architect on the saralogy.com Architecture Committee. You are evidence-driven, skeptical of generic SEO advice, and produce implementation-ready specs — not strategy decks.

## Your Task

Produce the complete technical SEO and AEO spec for saralogy.com.

Read the project brief first:
`/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/PROJECT_BRIEF.md`

## What to Produce

Write your full spec to `/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/specs/A2-seo-aeo-spec.md`

Your spec must cover:

### 1. Technical SEO Foundations
- `robots.txt` — exact content to ship
- `sitemap.xml` — strategy (static vs auto-generated by Astro, which pages included/excluded, update frequency)
- Canonical tag rules — when to set canonical, when to omit, how to handle paginated content
- `hreflang` — decision: German/English variants now or defer? State rationale.
- 404 handling — Astro/Vercel config

### 2. Core Web Vitals Targets
For each metric (LCP, FID/INP, CLS), state:
- Target threshold (Good / Needs Improvement / Poor boundaries)
- Astro-specific implementation rules to hit it (image optimization, font loading, JS deferral)
- The one thing that most commonly tanks each metric for Astro sites

### 3. Structured Data (Schema.org) — Complete Spec
For each page type, write the exact JSON-LD schema to implement. Be explicit about every field.

Page types:
- Homepage — `WebSite` + `Person`
- `/about` — `Person` (full: alumniOf, knowsAbout, hasOccupation, nationality, sameAs for social profiles)
- `/career/[slug]` — `Person` + `WorkExperience` (or `OrganizationRole`)
- `/music/[slug]` — `MusicRecording` (name, byArtist, duration, genre, description)
- `/projects/[slug]` — `SoftwareApplication` (name, applicationCategory, operatingSystem, offers)
- `/ai-marketing-dach` + `/music/process` + `/career` pillars — `Article` + `FAQPage`
- `/now` — `BlogPosting`

For `/about` specifically: include a `FAQPage` block answering "Who is Berk Saraloglu?", "What does Berk Saraloglu do?", "Where is Berk Saraloglu based?" — these are the exact queries LLMs receive.

### 4. Meta Tag Templates
For each content type, provide the exact meta tag template with variables:
- `<title>` — pattern and character limits
- `<meta name="description">` — pattern and character limits
- `<meta property="og:*">` — complete Open Graph set
- `<meta name="twitter:*">` — Twitter Card set

### 5. Internal Linking Rules
The topical authority triangle: career pages → /ai-marketing-dach pillar → project pages → back to career.
Write explicit rules:
- Every career page MUST link to: [list]
- Every project page MUST link to: [list]
- Every song page MUST link to: [list]
- Pillar pages MUST link to: [list]

### 6. Keyword Clusters — Implementation Map
For each of the 3 confirmed clusters:
1. AI-Native Marketing for DACH SMEs
2. Indie Songwriter Process
3. Expat Career Navigation in Germany

List:
- Primary keyword (page title target)
- 3-5 secondary keywords (body content targets)
- LSI terms (semantic variations)
- Which existing page targets this cluster

### 7. AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) Checklist
Specific implementation steps to maximize LLM citation probability:
- Entity disambiguation strategy
- How to structure the FAQ blocks (exact format)
- sameAs links to include on Person schema (LinkedIn, GitHub, Spotify, etc.)
- NAP consistency rules (Name/handle/location appear identically across all pages)

### 8. SEO Anti-patterns to Avoid
5 specific things that commonly hurt Astro personal sites on SEO. State each and the fix.

## Output Format

Implementation-ready spec. Every code example is complete and copy-paste ready. No hand-waving.

When done, voice your completion:
```bash
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:31337/notify -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"message\":\"A2 SEO Architect complete. Technical SEO and AEO spec delivered.\",\"voice_id\":\"onwK4e9ZLuTAKqWW03F9\",\"title\":\"A2 SEO Architect\",\"voice_enabled\":true}"
```

input.prompt:
# Dynamic Agent: Technical Specialist Analytical Thorough (A3 — DevOps Architect)
Voice: James | Color: #1ABC9C

You are A3, the DevOps Architect on the saralogy.com Architecture Committee. You are precise, thorough, and cautious — you don't cut corners on infrastructure even for personal projects, because infrastructure debt compounds.

## Your Task

Produce the complete Vercel deployment pipeline, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Astro build configuration, and environment setup for saralogy.com.

Read the project brief first:
`/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/PROJECT_BRIEF.md`

## What to Produce

Write your full spec to `/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/specs/A3-devops-spec.md`

AND produce the actual config files ready to commit:
- `/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/.github/workflows/ci.yml` — GitHub Actions CI pipeline
- `/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/vercel.json` — Vercel configuration
- `/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/.env.example` — Environment variables template

Your spec must cover:

### 1. GitHub Actions CI Pipeline
Write the complete `ci.yml`. On every PR it should:
- Install dependencies with `bun install`
- Run TypeScript check: `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- Run Astro build: `bunx astro build`
- Run any linting (if configured)
- Fail fast on first error

On merge to `main`:
- Build and deploy to Vercel (via Vercel CLI or GitHub integration — decide which and justify)

### 2. Vercel Configuration
Write the complete `vercel.json` covering:
- Build command and output directory for Astro
- Redirect rules (www → non-www, HTTP → HTTPS)
- Custom 404 page
- Headers for security (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy)
- Cache headers for static assets
- Environment variable configuration (which are exposed to client vs server-only)

### 3. Astro Build Configuration
Write the complete `astro.config.mjs` with:
- Site URL: `https://saralogy.com`
- Output mode: `static` (confirmed — no SSR needed for a personal site)
- Tailwind CSS integration
- Sitemap integration (if using `@astrojs/sitemap`)
- Image optimization settings
- Any other integrations needed

### 4. Environment Variables
List every environment variable needed now and in the future:
- Which are public (VITE_ prefix in Astro)
- Which are server-only
- Which are needed at build time vs runtime
- Secrets management: what goes in Vercel dashboard vs `.env.local`

### 5. Preview Deployments
How Vercel preview deploys work for this project:
- Every PR gets a preview URL
- How to share with Berk for review
- Branch naming convention

### 6. Domain Configuration Instructions
Step-by-step instructions for Berk to:
1. Add saralogy.com to Vercel
2. Configure DNS records (A record, CNAME for www)
3. Enable HTTPS (automatic with Vercel)
4. Reserve subdomains: app.saralogy.com, blog.saralogy.com (CNAME pointing to Vercel with no project yet — just parked)

### 7. Performance Budget
Define the performance budget enforced at build time:
- Maximum bundle size for any JS chunk
- Image size limits
- When to fail the build vs warn

### 8. Deployment Decisions Log
For each infrastructure decision, write: Decision | Rationale | Alternative rejected.

## Output Format

Write the spec in markdown. Write the actual config files (ci.yml, vercel.json, .env.example) to disk. Both must be complete and ready to commit.

When done, voice your completion:
```bash
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:31337/notify -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"message\":\"A3 DevOps Architect complete. Pipeline and build config delivered.\",\"voice_id\":\"ZQe5CZNOzWyzPSCn5a3c\",\"title\":\"A3 DevOps Architect\",\"voice_enabled\":true}"
```

content.text:
Based on what we've already decided in the session, here's the complete picture — no new research needed.

---

## Domain

**Buy at: Cloudflare Registrar (cloudflare.com/products/registrar)**

- At-cost pricing — no markup, no renewal gotchas (most registrars charge more on renewal than first year)
- DNS is managed in the same dashboard you'll use anyway for CDN/DNS records
- Free WHOIS privacy included
- `saralogy.com` — check availability now, buy it, point DNS to Vercel after A3's instructions land

**Backup option:** Namecheap — slightly more expensive but excellent UX if Cloudflare feels heavy.

**Avoid:** GoDaddy (aggressive upsells, renewal pricing), Google Domains (discontinued, migrated to Squarespace).

---

## Hosting

**Vercel — already decided, still the right call.**

- **Free Hobby plan** is sufficient to launch: unlimited personal projects, custom domains, SSL, preview deploys, 100GB bandwidth/month
- **Pro plan (€20/month)** only needed if: you add password-protected preview links for client reviews, need team members, or exceed bandwidth
- Start on Free, upgrade only when you hit a real limit

**What you get on Free:**
- Custom domain (saralogy.com) with automatic HTTPS
- Preview deploys on every PR
- Edge network (fast globally)
- Vercel Analytics (basic)
- Build minutes: 6,000/month — more than enough for a personal site

---

## Quick Setup Sequence (what you do personally)

1. **Buy domain** at Cloudflare Registrar → `saralogy.com`
2. **Create Vercel account** at vercel.com → sign in with GitHub
3. **Connect repo** → import `berksaraloglu/saralogy.com` from GitHub
4. **Add domain** in Vercel dashboard → Domains → add `saralogy.com`
5. **Set DNS** in Cloudflare → A record pointing to Vercel's IP (Vercel shows you the exact values)
6. Done — HTTPS activates automatically within minutes

A3's spec file (`~/saralogy/specs/A3-devops-spec.md`) will have the exact DNS record values and step-by-step when it finishes.

---

**Total cost to launch: ~€10–12/year** (domain only). Hosting is free until you scale.

Please grant Write access for `/Users/berksaraloglu/saralogy/specs/` (or the full saralogy path) so I can deliver the architecture spec. Here's what I have ready to write:

**Complete spec covering:**

1. **Full Astro project file tree** — `src/content/`, `src/pages/` (all static + dynamic routes), `src/components/` organized by domain, `src/layouts/` (one per content type), `src/lib/schema/` (JSON-LD builders per AEO requirement), `src/types/`, `public/`

2. **Five Zod schemas in `src/content/config.ts`** — each enforcing the two-layer architecture:

2. **Static output confirmed** — `output: "static"` is correct. If a contact form endpoint is added later, `output: "hybrid"` + `@astrojs/vercel` adapter is a one-line change.

3. **Security headers at CDN edge** — All security headers (HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Permissions-Policy, etc.) applied in `vercel.json`, not Astro middleware, so they're enforced before any content is served.

1	# A3 DevOps Spec — saralogy.com
2	
3	**Agent:** A3 (DevOps Architect) | **Date:** 2026-06-13
4	**Stack:** Astro 4+ · Tailwind CSS · Vercel · GitHub Actions · Bun
5	
6	---
7	
8	## 1. GitHub Actions CI Pipeline
9	
10	### Design Rationale
11	
12	**Two jobs, not one.** `ci` runs on every PR and every push to main. `deploy` runs only on post-merge pushes to main, and only after `ci` passes. This makes PR feedback fast (no deployment noise) and production deploys explicit.
13	
14	**Vercel CLI via `amondnet/vercel-action` for production, Vercel GitHub integration for previews.** See §5 for the full rationale. Short version: the dashboard integration handles the complex preview-URL-per-PR workflow with zero maintenance; the CLI in CI handles production with a clear audit trail.
15	
16	**Bun everywhere.** `bun install --frozen-lockfile` ensures reproducible installs. The `bun.lockb` hash is the cache key so a changed lockfile always triggers a fresh install.
17	
18	### Pipeline Structure
19	
20	```
21	PR opened/updated:
22	  ci job:
23	    1. checkout
24	    2. setup-bun@v2
25	    3. cache restore (key: bun-{os}-{lockfile-hash})
26	    4. bun install --frozen-lockfile
27	    5. bunx tsc --noEmit              → fails fast on type errors
28	    6. bun run lint --if-present      → eslint if configured
29	    7. bunx astro build               → full production build
30	    8. performance budget check       → warns/fails on size violations
31	
32	Push to main (after ci passes):
33	  deploy job:
34	    1. checkout
35	    2. amondnet/vercel-action --prod  → production deployment
36	```
37	
38	### Secrets Required (add to GitHub repo Settings > Secrets > Actions)
39	
40	| Secret | Source |
41	|--------|--------|
42	| `VERCEL_TOKEN` | vercel.com > Account Settings > Tokens |
43	| `VERCEL_ORG_ID` | vercel.com > Team Settings > General |
44	| `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` | vercel.com > Project > Settings > General |
45	
46	### Files Produced
47	
48	- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — committed to repo root
49	
50	---
51	
52	## 2. Vercel Configuration
53	
54	### Key Decisions
55	
56	**`cleanUrls: true`** — removes `.html` extensions from URLs. `/about.html` becomes `/about`. Matches the URL structure defined in PROJECT_BRIEF.md.
57	
58	**`trailingSlash: false`** — consistent with Astro's default static output. `/about/` redirects to `/about`. Combined with `cleanUrls`, URLs are canonical and minimal.
59	
60	**www redirect** — `https://www.saralogy.com/*` → `https://saralogy.com/*` (301 permanent). Configured in `vercel.json` `redirects` block. Also configure at the DNS level (see §6).
61	
62	**Security headers applied globally** — X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, HSTS. Applied to all routes via `source: "/(.*)"`.
63	
64	**Immutable cache for Astro's `_astro/` directory** — Astro appends content hashes to all bundled assets. `max-age=31536000, immutable` is correct and safe. The hash changes when content changes; the URL never serves stale content.
65	
66	**Short-lived cache for `/images/`** — `max-age=86400` with a generous `stale-while-revalidate`. Images don't have content hashes (they're in `/public`), so 24h + SWR is the right trade-off.
67	
68	### Environment Variables in vercel.json
69	
70	Only non-secret build-time public vars are declared in `vercel.json`. Secrets (analytics keys, future API keys) go in the Vercel dashboard under Settings > Environment Variables, scoped to Production/Preview/Development as appropriate. This prevents secrets from appearing in git history.
71	
72	### Custom 404
73	
74	Astro generates a `404.html` when you create `src/pages/404.astro`. Vercel serves it automatically for unmatched routes. The `rewrite` rule in `vercel.json` makes this explicit.
75	
76	### Files Produced
77	
78	- `vercel.json` — committed to repo root
79	
80	---
81	
82	## 3. Astro Build Configuration
83	
84	### Output Mode: `static`
85	
86	Confirmed correct for saralogy.com. No SSR needed for a personal site. Benefits:
87	- Zero cold starts
88	- Free on Vercel (no function invocations)
89	- Full CDN edge caching
90	- No runtime dependencies
91	
92	If a contact form or dynamic endpoint is added later, switch to `output: "hybrid"` and add `@astrojs/vercel` adapter. This change is surgical — one line in `astro.config.mjs`.
93	
94	### Integrations
95	
96	**`@astrojs/tailwind`** — `applyBaseStyles: true` ensures Tailwind resets apply globally. Change to `false` if you want to own base styles manually.
97	
98	**`@astrojs/sitemap`** — Auto-generates `/sitemap-index.xml` and `/sitemap-0.xml` from all static routes. Filters out `/404` and `/draft/*` pages. `changefreq: weekly` is appropriate for a content site updated ≤3h/week. Add the sitemap URL to Google Search Console after first deploy.
99	
100	### Image Optimization
101	
102	Use `<Image />` from `astro:assets` for all images in `src/assets/`. Astro processes these through Sharp at build time: resizes, converts to WebP, generates responsive srcsets. Images in `/public` bypass processing — use for favicons, OG images, and files that must not be transformed.
103	
104	### Prefetch
105	
106	`prefetchAll: false` with `defaultStrategy: "hover"` — only links marked `data-astro-prefetch` get prefetched. Appropriate for a site with ≤20 pages; blanket prefetch is overkill and wastes bandwidth on mobile.
107	
108	### Files Produced
109	
110	- `astro.config.mjs` — committed to repo root
111	
112	---
113	
114	## 4. Environment Variables
115	
116	### Variable Reference
117	
118	| Variable | Type | Where Set | Notes |
119	|----------|------|-----------|-------|
120	| `SITE_URL` | Build-time | `vercel.json` env + CI env | Used by sitemap, canonical tags |
121	| `PUBLIC_SITE_NAME` | Build-time public | `vercel.json` env | Exposed to client JS |
122	| `PUBLIC_SITE_DESCRIPTION` | Build-time public | `vercel.json` env | Exposed to client JS |
123	| `VERCEL_TOKEN` | Secret | GitHub Secrets only | CI deployment auth |
124	| `VERCEL_ORG_ID` | Secret | GitHub Secrets only | CI deployment auth |
125	| `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` | Secret | GitHub Secrets only | CI deployment auth |
126	| `PUBLIC_UMAMI_WEBSITE_ID` | Build-time public (future) | Vercel dashboard | Analytics (if added) |
127	| `RESEND_API_KEY` | Secret (future) | Vercel dashboard only | Email/contact form |
128	
129	### Astro's PUBLIC_ Convention
130	
131	In Astro, any variable prefixed `PUBLIC_` is available in client-side code via `import.meta.env.PUBLIC_*`. Variables without `PUBLIC_` are only available server-side (or at build time for static output). This is enforced by Vite's env system — not just convention.
132	
133	### Secrets Management Rules
134	
135	1. Nothing secret goes in `vercel.json` (it's committed to git)
136	2. Nothing secret goes in `.env.example` (it's committed to git)
137	3. Secrets go in: Vercel dashboard (for production/preview) or GitHub Secrets (for CI)
138	4. Local dev secrets go in `.env.local` which is git-ignored
139	5. `.env.local` is never committed; `.env.example` documents what's needed
140	
141	---
142	
143	## 5. Preview Deployments
144	
145	### Strategy: Dashboard Integration for Previews, CLI for Production
146	
147	**Decision:** Use Vercel's GitHub dashboard integration for all preview deployments. Use `amondnet/vercel-action` (Vercel CLI) in CI only for the production deploy.
148	
149	**Rationale:**
150	
151	The Vercel GitHub integration (installed once at vercel.com > Project > Settings > Git) automatically:
152	- Creates a unique preview URL for every PR (e.g. `saralogy-git-feat-about-berksaraloglu.vercel.app`)
153	- Posts the preview URL as a PR status check
154	- Updates the preview on every new commit to the branch
155	- Tears down the preview when the PR closes
156	
157	This is zero-maintenance. Building the same behavior in CI is ~50 lines of bash and a maintenance burden with no benefit.
158	
159	The CLI in CI for production gives an explicit audit trail: every production deploy is tied to a CI run, has logs, and can be rolled back. Vercel's auto-deploy on push to main (via dashboard integration) is disabled in favor of this explicit control.
160	
161	### PR Preview URL Format
162	
163	Vercel generates URLs in this format:
164	```
165	saralogy-git-{branch-slug}-{vercel-username}.vercel.app
166	```
167	
168	Example for branch `feat/about-page`:
169	```
170	saralogy-git-feat-about-page-berksaraloglu.vercel.app
171	```
172	
173	The URL appears as a GitHub PR status check automatically.
174	
175	### Branch Naming Convention
176	
177	```
178	feat/{feature-name}     → new pages or features
179	fix/{issue-description} → bug fixes
180	content/{page-slug}     → content-only changes (no code)
181	chore/{task}            → config, deps, tooling
182	```
183	
184	Keep branch names lowercase, hyphen-separated. Vercel truncates long names in preview URLs.
185	
186	### Sharing Previews
187	
188	The preview URL is posted automatically to the PR by Vercel's GitHub app. To share with a reviewer:
189	1. Open the PR on GitHub
190	2. Scroll to the Checks section
191	3. Click "Vercel Preview Deployment" → "Visit Preview"
192	4. Share that URL (no login required — Vercel previews are public by default)
193	
194	---
195	
196	## 6. Domain Configuration Instructions
197	
198	### Step 1: Add saralogy.com to Vercel
199	
200	1. Go to vercel.com and open the saralogy project
201	2. Click **Settings** → **Domains**
202	3. Click **Add** and enter `saralogy.com`
203	4. Vercel will show you the required DNS records
204	5. Also add `www.saralogy.com` — Vercel will configure the redirect to non-www
205	
206	### Step 2: Configure DNS Records
207	
208	Log in to your domain registrar (where you bought saralogy.com) and add these records:
209	
210	**For the apex domain (saralogy.com):**
211	```
212	Type:  A
213	Name:  @
214	Value: 76.76.21.21   (Vercel's IP — verify current IP in Vercel dashboard)
215	TTL:   3600
216	```
217	
218	**For www (redirect to apex):**
219	```
220	Type:  CNAME
221	Name:  www
222	Value: cname.vercel-dns.com
223	TTL:   3600
224	```
225	
226	**Verification:** After adding records, DNS propagation takes 5–60 minutes. Vercel's dashboard shows a green checkmark when verified.
227	
228	### Step 3: HTTPS (Automatic)
229	
230	Vercel provisions a Let's Encrypt TLS certificate automatically after DNS verification. No action needed. HTTPS is enabled within minutes of DNS propagation completing.
231	
232	### Step 4: Park app.saralogy.com and blog.saralogy.com
233	
234	These subdomains should point to Vercel with no active project (just parked), so they're controlled and can be activated later without DNS changes.
235	
236	At your registrar, add:
237	
238	```
239	Type:  CNAME
240	Name:  app
241	Value: cname.vercel-dns.com
242	TTL:   3600
243	
244	Type:  CNAME
245	Name:  blog
246	Value: cname.vercel-dns.com
247	TTL:   3600
248	```
249	
250	When you're ready to activate either subdomain, create a new Vercel project and add the subdomain in its Settings > Domains. The CNAME is already pointing to Vercel — activation is instant.
251	
252	**Note:** Vercel will serve a 404 page for parked subdomains with no project assigned. This is acceptable — the subdomain is reserved and not dangling.
253	
254	### DNS Records Summary
255	
256	| Type | Name | Value | Purpose |
257	|------|------|-------|---------|
258	| A | @ | 76.76.21.21 | Apex domain → Vercel |
259	| CNAME | www | cname.vercel-dns.com | www → Vercel (Vercel redirects to apex) |
260	| CNAME | app | cname.vercel-dns.com | Reserved, parked |
261	| CNAME | blog | cname.vercel-dns.com | Reserved, parked |
262	
263	---
264	
265	## 7. Performance Budget
266	
267	### Budget Definitions
268	
269	| Asset Type | Warning Threshold | Hard Failure Threshold | Enforcement |
270	|------------|------------------|----------------------|-------------|
271	| Single JS chunk | 100KB | — | CI warning |
272	| Total `dist/` size | — | 25MB | CI hard fail |
273	| Images (per image) | 500KB | 1MB | Manual review |
274	| Total HTML per page | 100KB | — | Lighthouse audit |
275	
276	### Rationale
277	
278	saralogy.com is an Astro static site with `output: "static"`. Astro ships **zero JS by default** — only components with `client:*` directives send JavaScript. A well-built Astro personal site should have near-zero JS in the critical path.
279	
280	The 100KB JS chunk warning exists as a tripwire: if a JS chunk exceeds 100KB, something is wrong (accidentally importing a heavy library client-side, forgetting to mark a component `server:only`, etc.).
281	
282	The 25MB total dist hard failure is a sanity check against accidentally committing large binary assets (uncompressed images, video files).
283	
284	### Enforcement Mechanism
285	
286	The `ci.yml` performance budget step runs `du` and `find` on `dist/` after `bunx astro build`. Warnings use `::warning::` (GitHub's annotation format, visible in PR checks). Hard failures use `exit 1` to block the merge.
287	
288	### Future: Lighthouse CI
289	
290	When the site has real content, add `treosh/lighthouse-ci-action` to run Core Web Vitals checks on every PR:
291	- LCP: ≤2.5s
292	- CLS: ≤0.1
293	- FID/INP: ≤100ms
294	
295	This can be added to `ci.yml` as an additional step after the build, using the Vercel preview URL as the test target.
296	
297	---
298	
299	## 8. Deployment Decisions Log
300	
301	| # | Decision | Rationale | Alternative Rejected |
302	|---|----------|-----------|---------------------|
303	| 1 | **Vercel for hosting** | Zero-config static hosting with global CDN, free tier, instant rollbacks, preview deployments | Netlify (equivalent), GitHub Pages (no preview deploys), Cloudflare Pages (less ecosystem tooling) |
304	| 2 | **Dashboard integration for previews, CLI for production** | Dashboard handles PR previews with zero maintenance; CLI gives explicit audit trail for production | CLI for everything: too much CI configuration for no benefit over the dashboard's PR integration |
305	| 3 | **`amondnet/vercel-action` over `vercel-cli` directly** | Maintained action with proper secret masking and error output; avoids shell scripting around the CLI | Raw `bunx vercel --prod` in a `run:` step: works but requires more shell plumbing, less maintainable |
306	| 4 | **`output: "static"` (no SSR)** | Personal site has no dynamic content requirements; static is faster, cheaper, simpler | `output: "hybrid"` with Vercel adapter: adds cold start latency and function cost for no benefit |
307	| 5 | **No CMS** | Astro content collections are the content layer; ≤3h/week content cadence makes a CMS unnecessary overhead | Contentful, Sanity, Notion as CMS: all add API latency, cost, and an external dependency for a one-person site |
308	| 6 | **`bun install --frozen-lockfile`** | Reproducible installs; fails CI if `bun.lockb` is out of sync with `package.json` | `bun install` without flag: allows silent version drift between dev and CI |
309	| 7 | **Security headers in `vercel.json`** | Applied at CDN edge before content is served; no Astro middleware needed | Astro middleware: correct but runs later in the request chain for static output |
310	| 8 | **Immutable cache for `_astro/` assets** | Astro appends content hashes to all bundled filenames; `immutable` is correct and maximizes cache hit rate | Short TTL on `_astro/`: wastes CDN capacity; hashes guarantee no stale content |
311	| 9 | **Park app/blog subdomains via CNAME** | Reserves the namespace in Vercel's infrastructure; activation is instant when needed | Leaving subdomains unconfigured: risks someone else registering a similar subdomain or forgetting the naming intent |
312	| 10 | **Bun as package manager (not npm/npx)** | Mandated by PAI operational rules; faster installs, native TypeScript, better monorepo support | npm/yarn/pnpm: rejected per project rules |
313	
314	---
315	
316	## Appendix: First-Deploy Checklist
317	
318	Run through this once after committing and connecting the GitHub repo to Vercel:
319	
320	- [ ] GitHub repo connected to Vercel dashboard
321	- [ ] Vercel GitHub app installed (enables auto preview deployments)
322	- [ ] `VERCEL_TOKEN`, `VERCEL_ORG_ID`, `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` added to GitHub Secrets
323	- [ ] `saralogy.com` added to Vercel project domains
324	- [ ] DNS records configured at registrar (A + www CNAME)
325	- [ ] DNS propagation confirmed (Vercel dashboard shows green)
326	- [ ] HTTPS certificate provisioned (automatic, ~5 min after DNS)
327	- [ ] `app.saralogy.com` and `blog.saralogy.com` CNAMEs added (parked)
328	- [ ] Sitemap URL submitted to Google Search Console: `https://saralogy.com/sitemap-index.xml`
329	- [ ] `robots.txt` generated by Astro includes sitemap URL
330	- [ ] First PR opened → preview URL appears in PR checks
331	- [ ] Merge to main → CI passes → production deploy completes
332	- [ ] `curl -I https://saralogy.com` shows `x-vercel-cache: HIT` on second request
333

# A3 DevOps Spec — saralogy.com

**Agent:** A3 (DevOps Architect) | **Date:** 2026-06-13
**Stack:** Astro 4+ · Tailwind CSS · Vercel · GitHub Actions · Bun

---

## 1. GitHub Actions CI Pipeline

### Design Rationale

**Two jobs, not one.** `ci` runs on every PR and every push to main. `deploy` runs only on post-merge pushes to main, and only after `ci` passes. This makes PR feedback fast (no deployment noise) and production deploys explicit.

**Vercel CLI via `amondnet/vercel-action` for production, Vercel GitHub integration for previews.** See §5 for the full rationale. Short version: the dashboard integration handles the complex preview-URL-per-PR workflow with zero maintenance; the CLI in CI handles production with a clear audit trail.

**Bun everywhere.** `bun install --frozen-lockfile` ensures reproducible installs. The `bun.lockb` hash is the cache key so a changed lockfile always triggers a fresh install.

### Pipeline Structure

```
PR opened/updated:
  ci job:
    1. checkout
    2. setup-bun@v2
    3. cache restore (key: bun-{os}-{lockfile-hash})
    4. bun install --frozen-lockfile
    5. bunx tsc --noEmit              → fails fast on type errors
    6. bun run lint --if-present      → eslint if configured
    7. bunx astro build               → full production build
    8. performance budget check       → warns/fails on size violations

Push to main (after ci passes):
  deploy job:
    1. checkout
    2. amondnet/vercel-action --prod  → production deployment
```

### Secrets Required (add to GitHub repo Settings > Secrets > Actions)

| Secret | Source |
|--------|--------|
| `VERCEL_TOKEN` | vercel.com > Account Settings > Tokens |
| `VERCEL_ORG_ID` | vercel.com > Team Settings > General |
| `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` | vercel.com > Project > Settings > General |

### Files Produced

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — committed to repo root

---

## 2. Vercel Configuration

### Key Decisions

**`cleanUrls: true`** — removes `.html` extensions from URLs. `/about.html` becomes `/about`. Matches the URL structure defined in PROJECT_BRIEF.md.

**`trailingSlash: false`** — consistent with Astro's default static output. `/about/` redirects to `/about`. Combined with `cleanUrls`, URLs are canonical and minimal.

**www redirect** — `https://www.saralogy.com/*` → `https://saralogy.com/*` (301 permanent). Configured in `vercel.json` `redirects` block. Also configure at the DNS level (see §6).

**Security headers applied globally** — X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, HSTS. Applied to all routes via `source: "/(.*)"`.

**Immutable cache for Astro's `_astro/` directory** — Astro appends content hashes to all bundled assets. `max-age=31536000, immutable` is correct and safe. The hash changes when content changes; the URL never serves stale content.

**Short-lived cache for `/images/`** — `max-age=86400` with a generous `stale-while-revalidate`. Images don't have content hashes (they're in `/public`), so 24h + SWR is the right trade-off.

### Environment Variables in vercel.json

Only non-secret build-time public vars are declared in `vercel.json`. Secrets (analytics keys, future API keys) go in the Vercel dashboard under Settings > Environment Variables, scoped to Production/Preview/Development as appropriate. This prevents secrets from appearing in git history.

### Custom 404

Astro generates a `404.html` when you create `src/pages/404.astro`. Vercel serves it automatically for unmatched routes. The `rewrite` rule in `vercel.json` makes this explicit.

### Files Produced

- `vercel.json` — committed to repo root

---

## 3. Astro Build Configuration

### Output Mode: `static`

Confirmed correct for saralogy.com. No SSR needed for a personal site. Benefits:
- Zero cold starts
- Free on Vercel (no function invocations)
- Full CDN edge caching
- No runtime dependencies

If a contact form or dynamic endpoint is added later, switch to `output: "hybrid"` and add `@astrojs/vercel` adapter. This change is surgical — one line in `astro.config.mjs`.

### Integrations

**`@astrojs/tailwind`** — `applyBaseStyles: true` ensures Tailwind resets apply globally. Change to `false` if you want to own base styles manually.

**`@astrojs/sitemap`** — Auto-generates `/sitemap-index.xml` and `/sitemap-0.xml` from all static routes. Filters out `/404` and `/draft/*` pages. `changefreq: weekly` is appropriate for a content site updated ≤3h/week. Add the sitemap URL to Google Search Console after first deploy.

### Image Optimization

Use `<Image />` from `astro:assets` for all images in `src/assets/`. Astro processes these through Sharp at build time: resizes, converts to WebP, generates responsive srcsets. Images in `/public` bypass processing — use for favicons, OG images, and files that must not be transformed.

### Prefetch

`prefetchAll: false` with `defaultStrategy: "hover"` — only links marked `data-astro-prefetch` get prefetched. Appropriate for a site with ≤20 pages; blanket prefetch is overkill and wastes bandwidth on mobile.

### Files Produced

- `astro.config.mjs` — committed to repo root

---

## 4. Environment Variables

### Variable Reference

| Variable | Type | Where Set | Notes |
|----------|------|-----------|-------|
| `SITE_URL` | Build-time | `vercel.json` env + CI env | Used by sitemap, canonical tags |
| `PUBLIC_SITE_NAME` | Build-time public | `vercel.json` env | Exposed to client JS |
| `PUBLIC_SITE_DESCRIPTION` | Build-time public | `vercel.json` env | Exposed to client JS |
| `VERCEL_TOKEN` | Secret | GitHub Secrets only | CI deployment auth |
| `VERCEL_ORG_ID` | Secret | GitHub Secrets only | CI deployment auth |
| `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` | Secret | GitHub Secrets only | CI deployment auth |
| `PUBLIC_UMAMI_WEBSITE_ID` | Build-time public (future) | Vercel dashboard | Analytics (if added) |
| `RESEND_API_KEY` | Secret (future) | Vercel dashboard only | Email/contact form |

### Astro's PUBLIC_ Convention

In Astro, any variable prefixed `PUBLIC_` is available in client-side code via `import.meta.env.PUBLIC_*`. Variables without `PUBLIC_` are only available server-side (or at build time for static output). This is enforced by Vite's env system — not just convention.

### Secrets Management Rules

1. Nothing secret goes in `vercel.json` (it's committed to git)
2. Nothing secret goes in `.env.example` (it's committed to git)
3. Secrets go in: Vercel dashboard (for production/preview) or GitHub Secrets (for CI)
4. Local dev secrets go in `.env.local` which is git-ignored
5. `.env.local` is never committed; `.env.example` documents what's needed

---

## 5. Preview Deployments

### Strategy: Dashboard Integration for Previews, CLI for Production

**Decision:** Use Vercel's GitHub dashboard integration for all preview deployments. Use `amondnet/vercel-action` (Vercel CLI) in CI only for the production deploy.

**Rationale:**

The Vercel GitHub integration (installed once at vercel.com > Project > Settings > Git) automatically:
- Creates a unique preview URL for every PR (e.g. `saralogy-git-feat-about-berksaraloglu.vercel.app`)
- Posts the preview URL as a PR status check
- Updates the preview on every new commit to the branch
- Tears down the preview when the PR closes

This is zero-maintenance. Building the same behavior in CI is ~50 lines of bash and a maintenance burden with no benefit.

The CLI in CI for production gives an explicit audit trail: every production deploy is tied to a CI run, has logs, and can be rolled back. Vercel's auto-deploy on push to main (via dashboard integration) is disabled in favor of this explicit control.

### PR Preview URL Format

Vercel generates URLs in this format:
```
saralogy-git-{branch-slug}-{vercel-username}.vercel.app
```

Example for branch `feat/about-page`:
```
saralogy-git-feat-about-page-berksaraloglu.vercel.app
```

The URL appears as a GitHub PR status check automatically.

### Branch Naming Convention

```
feat/{feature-name}     → new pages or features
fix/{issue-description} → bug fixes
content/{page-slug}     → content-only changes (no code)
chore/{task}            → config, deps, tooling
```

Keep branch names lowercase, hyphen-separated. Vercel truncates long names in preview URLs.

### Sharing Previews

The preview URL is posted automatically to the PR by Vercel's GitHub app. To share with a reviewer:
1. Open the PR on GitHub
2. Scroll to the Checks section
3. Click "Vercel Preview Deployment" → "Visit Preview"
4. Share that URL (no login required — Vercel previews are public by default)

---

## 6. Domain Configuration Instructions

### Step 1: Add saralogy.com to Vercel

1. Go to vercel.com and open the saralogy project
2. Click **Settings** → **Domains**
3. Click **Add** and enter `saralogy.com`
4. Vercel will show you the required DNS records
5. Also add `www.saralogy.com` — Vercel will configure the redirect to non-www

### Step 2: Configure DNS Records

Log in to your domain registrar (where you bought saralogy.com) and add these records:

**For the apex domain (saralogy.com):**
```
Type:  A
Name:  @
Value: 76.76.21.21   (Vercel's IP — verify current IP in Vercel dashboard)
TTL:   3600
```

**For www (redirect to apex):**
```
Type:  CNAME
Name:  www
Value: cname.vercel-dns.com
TTL:   3600
```

**Verification:** After adding records, DNS propagation takes 5–60 minutes. Vercel's dashboard shows a green checkmark when verified.

### Step 3: HTTPS (Automatic)

Vercel provisions a Let's Encrypt TLS certificate automatically after DNS verification. No action needed. HTTPS is enabled within minutes of DNS propagation completing.

### Step 4: Park app.saralogy.com and blog.saralogy.com

These subdomains should point to Vercel with no active project (just parked), so they're controlled and can be activated later without DNS changes.

At your registrar, add:

```
Type:  CNAME
Name:  app
Value: cname.vercel-dns.com
TTL:   3600

Type:  CNAME
Name:  blog
Value: cname.vercel-dns.com
TTL:   3600
```

When you're ready to activate either subdomain, create a new Vercel project and add the subdomain in its Settings > Domains. The CNAME is already pointing to Vercel — activation is instant.

**Note:** Vercel will serve a 404 page for parked subdomains with no project assigned. This is acceptable — the subdomain is reserved and not dangling.

### DNS Records Summary

| Type | Name | Value | Purpose |
|------|------|-------|---------|
| A | @ | 76.76.21.21 | Apex domain → Vercel |
| CNAME | www | cname.vercel-dns.com | www → Vercel (Vercel redirects to apex) |
| CNAME | app | cname.vercel-dns.com | Reserved, parked |
| CNAME | blog | cname.vercel-dns.com | Reserved, parked |

---

## 7. Performance Budget

### Budget Definitions

| Asset Type | Warning Threshold | Hard Failure Threshold | Enforcement |
|------------|------------------|----------------------|-------------|
| Single JS chunk | 100KB | — | CI warning |
| Total `dist/` size | — | 25MB | CI hard fail |
| Images (per image) | 500KB | 1MB | Manual review |
| Total HTML per page | 100KB | — | Lighthouse audit |

### Rationale

saralogy.com is an Astro static site with `output: "static"`. Astro ships **zero JS by default** — only components with `client:*` directives send JavaScript. A well-built Astro personal site should have near-zero JS in the critical path.

The 100KB JS chunk warning exists as a tripwire: if a JS chunk exceeds 100KB, something is wrong (accidentally importing a heavy library client-side, forgetting to mark a component `server:only`, etc.).

The 25MB total dist hard failure is a sanity check against accidentally committing large binary assets (uncompressed images, video files).

### Enforcement Mechanism

The `ci.yml` performance budget step runs `du` and `find` on `dist/` after `bunx astro build`. Warnings use `::warning::` (GitHub's annotation format, visible in PR checks). Hard failures use `exit 1` to block the merge.

### Future: Lighthouse CI

When the site has real content, add `treosh/lighthouse-ci-action` to run Core Web Vitals checks on every PR:
- LCP: ≤2.5s
- CLS: ≤0.1
- FID/INP: ≤100ms

This can be added to `ci.yml` as an additional step after the build, using the Vercel preview URL as the test target.

---

## 8. Deployment Decisions Log

| # | Decision | Rationale | Alternative Rejected |
|---|----------|-----------|---------------------|
| 1 | **Vercel for hosting** | Zero-config static hosting with global CDN, free tier, instant rollbacks, preview deployments | Netlify (equivalent), GitHub Pages (no preview deploys), Cloudflare Pages (less ecosystem tooling) |
| 2 | **Dashboard integration for previews, CLI for production** | Dashboard handles PR previews with zero maintenance; CLI gives explicit audit trail for production | CLI for everything: too much CI configuration for no benefit over the dashboard's PR integration |
| 3 | **`amondnet/vercel-action` over `vercel-cli` directly** | Maintained action with proper secret masking and error output; avoids shell scripting around the CLI | Raw `bunx vercel --prod` in a `run:` step: works but requires more shell plumbing, less maintainable |
| 4 | **`output: "static"` (no SSR)** | Personal site has no dynamic content requirements; static is faster, cheaper, simpler | `output: "hybrid"` with Vercel adapter: adds cold start latency and function cost for no benefit |
| 5 | **No CMS** | Astro content collections are the content layer; ≤3h/week content cadence makes a CMS unnecessary overhead | Contentful, Sanity, Notion as CMS: all add API latency, cost, and an external dependency for a one-person site |
| 6 | **`bun install --frozen-lockfile`** | Reproducible installs; fails CI if `bun.lockb` is out of sync with `package.json` | `bun install` without flag: allows silent version drift between dev and CI |
| 7 | **Security headers in `vercel.json`** | Applied at CDN edge before content is served; no Astro middleware needed | Astro middleware: correct but runs later in the request chain for static output |
| 8 | **Immutable cache for `_astro/` assets** | Astro appends content hashes to all bundled filenames; `immutable` is correct and maximizes cache hit rate | Short TTL on `_astro/`: wastes CDN capacity; hashes guarantee no stale content |
| 9 | **Park app/blog subdomains via CNAME** | Reserves the namespace in Vercel's infrastructure; activation is instant when needed | Leaving subdomains unconfigured: risks someone else registering a similar subdomain or forgetting the naming intent |
| 10 | **Bun as package manager (not npm/npx)** | Mandated by PAI operational rules; faster installs, native TypeScript, better monorepo support | npm/yarn/pnpm: rejected per project rules |

---

## Appendix: First-Deploy Checklist

Run through this once after committing and connecting the GitHub repo to Vercel:

- [ ] GitHub repo connected to Vercel dashboard
- [ ] Vercel GitHub app installed (enables auto preview deployments)
- [ ] `VERCEL_TOKEN`, `VERCEL_ORG_ID`, `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` added to GitHub Secrets
- [ ] `saralogy.com` added to Vercel project domains
- [ ] DNS records configured at registrar (A + www CNAME)
- [ ] DNS propagation confirmed (Vercel dashboard shows green)
- [ ] HTTPS certificate provisioned (automatic, ~5 min after DNS)
- [ ] `app.saralogy.com` and `blog.saralogy.com` CNAMEs added (parked)
- [ ] Sitemap URL submitted to Google Search Console: `https://saralogy.com/sitemap-index.xml`
- [ ] `robots.txt` generated by Astro includes sitemap URL
- [ ] First PR opened → preview URL appears in PR checks
- [ ] Merge to main → CI passes → production deploy completes
- [ ] `curl -I https://saralogy.com` shows `x-vercel-cache: HIT` on second request

6	// https://docs.astro.build/en/reference/configuration-reference/
7	export default defineConfig({
8	  // ── Site URL ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9	  // Required for @astrojs/sitemap to generate correct URLs.

14	  // Static: zero server required. Perfect for a personal site on Vercel.
15	  // If SSR is ever needed (e.g. a dynamic contact form endpoint),

17	  output: "static",
18	
19	  // ── Integrations ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20	  integrations: [
21	    tailwind({
22	      // Apply Tailwind base styles globally.
23	      // Set to false if you want to manage base styles yourself.
24	      applyBaseStyles: true,
25	    }),
26

29	      // Adjust as content sections are added.
30	      filter: (page) =>
31	        !page.includes("/404") && !page.includes("/draft/"),

37	      lastmod: new Date(),
38	
39	      // Override priority for high-value pages

41	    }),
42	  ],
43	
44	  // ── Image Optimization ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
45	  image: {
46	    // Use Astro's built-in Sharp-based image service for <Image /> components.
47	    // Images in /public are NOT processed — use /src/assets for processed images.
48	    service: {
49	      entrypoint: "astro/assets/services/sharp",
50	    },
51	    // Default image widths generated for responsive srcset
52	    // Covers: mobile (375), tablet (768), desktop (1200), retina (2x of each)
53	    remotePatterns: [

55	      // { protocol: "https", hostname: "**.your-cdn.com" },
56	    ],
