input.content:
# A1 Systems Architecture — saralogy.com

**Agent:** A1 (Systems Architect) | **Date:** 2026-06-13
**Stack:** Astro 4+ · TypeScript · Tailwind CSS · Vercel

---

## 1. Astro Project Directory Structure

```
saralogy/
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       └── ci.yml                    # CI/CD pipeline (A3)
├── public/
│   ├── favicon.ico
│   ├── favicon.svg
│   ├── robots.txt                    # Generated by Astro sitemap or manual
│   ├── og-default.png                # Default OG image (1200×630)
│   └── fonts/                        # Self-hosted fonts (if any)
├── src/
│   ├── assets/                       # Processed by Astro Sharp (use <Image />)
│   │   ├── images/
│   │   │   ├── career/               # Company logos, work photos
│   │   │   ├── music/                # Song artwork, live photos
│   │   │   ├── projects/             # Project screenshots, demos
│   │   │   └── trips/                # Travel photos
│   │   └── profile/
│   │       └── berk-saraloglu.jpg    # Primary portrait
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── layout/
│   │   │   ├── Header.astro
│   │   │   ├── Footer.astro
│   │   │   ├── Nav.astro
│   │   │   └── IntentRouter.astro    # Homepage audience routing component
│   │   ├── career/
│   │   │   ├── TenureCard.astro      # Career index card
│   │   │   ├── PermanentLayer.astro  # Permanent section of tenure page
│   │   │   └── LivingLayer.astro     # Living/updatable section
│   │   ├── music/
│   │   │   ├── SongCard.astro
│   │   │   ├── EmbedPlayer.astro     # YouTube/Spotify embed wrapper
│   │   │   └── SongIndex.astro
│   │   ├── projects/
│   │   │   ├── ProjectCard.astro
│   │   │   └── StatusBadge.astro     # concept/mvp/beta/live
│   │   ├── trips/
│   │   │   ├── TripCard.astro
│   │   │   └── CountryMap.astro      # SVG world map (visited countries)
│   │   ├── writing/
│   │   │   ├── EssayCard.astro
│   │   │   └── IntersectionTag.astro # Domain intersection tags
│   │   └── seo/
│   │       ├── JsonLd.astro          # JSON-LD schema injector
│   │       ├── MetaTags.astro        # OG + Twitter Card + canonical
│   │       └── FAQBlock.astro        # AEO FAQ component
│   ├── content/
│   │   ├── config.ts                 # Zod schemas for all collections
│   │   ├── career/
│   │   │   ├── bsh.mdx               # BSH / Siemens Global
│   │   │   ├── vesync.mdx            # VeSync GmbH
│   │   │   ├── samsung.mdx           # Samsung Electronics
│   │   │   └── orma.mdx              # ORMA Chipwood
│   │   ├── music/
│   │   │   └── [song-slug].mdx       # One file per song
│   │   ├── projects/
│   │   │   ├── vision-intelligence.mdx
│   │   │   └── resume-matcher.mdx
│   │   ├── trips/
│   │   │   └── [country].mdx         # country-level entries
│   │   └── writing/
│   │       └── [essay-slug].mdx      # The Intersection essays
│   ├── layouts/
│   │   ├── Base.astro                # HTML shell, head, fonts, globals
│   │   ├── Page.astro                # Generic content page
│   │   ├── Tenure.astro              # Career tenure two-layer layout
│   │   ├── Song.astro                # Song archaeology layout
│   │   ├── Project.astro             # Project pitch layout
│   │   ├── Trip.astro                # Trip essay layout
│   │   └── Essay.astro               # Writing/Intersection essay layout
│   ├── lib/
│   │   └── schema/
│   │       ├── person.ts             # Person JSON-LD builder
│   │       ├── workExperience.ts     # WorkExperience JSON-LD builder
│   │       ├── musicRecording.ts     # MusicRecording JSON-LD builder
│   │       ├── softwareApp.ts        # SoftwareApplication JSON-LD builder
│   │       ├── article.ts            # Article JSON-LD builder
│   │       └── faq.ts                # FAQPage JSON-LD builder
│   ├── pages/
│   │   ├── index.astro               # Homepage / intent router
│   │   ├── about.astro               # Entity page (LLM-optimized)
│   │   ├── work.astro                # Availability + hire me
│   │   ├── now.astro                 # Monthly update page
│   │   ├── 404.astro                 # Custom 404
│   │   ├── career/
│   │   │   ├── index.astro           # Career index (timeline view)
│   │   │   └── [slug].astro          # Dynamic tenure pages
│   │   ├── music/
│   │   │   ├── index.astro           # Music hub
│   │   │   ├── process.astro         # Songwriting process (static)
│   │   │   ├── gear.astro            # Gear page (static)
│   │   │   └── [slug].astro          # Dynamic song archaeology pages
│   │   ├── projects/
│   │   │   ├── index.astro           # Projects index
│   │   │   └── [slug].astro          # Dynamic project pages
│   │   ├── trips/
│   │   │   ├── index.astro           # Trip index + world map
│   │   │   └── [slug].astro          # Dynamic country/city pages
│   │   ├── writing/
│   │   │   ├── index.astro           # Essays index
│   │   │   └── [slug].astro          # Dynamic essay pages
│   │   └── ai-marketing-dach.astro   # Pillar page — Cluster 1 (static, long-form)
│   ├── styles/
│   │   └── global.css                # Tailwind directives + CSS custom properties
│   └── types/
│       └── index.ts                  # Shared TypeScript types
├── astro.config.mjs                  # Astro config (A3)
├── vercel.json                       # Vercel config (A3)
├── tsconfig.json
├── tailwind.config.mjs
├── package.json
├── bun.lockb
├── .env.example                      # (A3)
└── .gitignore
```

---

## 2. Content Collection Zod Schemas (`src/content/config.ts`)

```typescript
import { defineCollection, z } from "astro:content";

// ── Shared sub-schemas ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

const seoFields = z.object({
  title: z.string().max(60),
  description: z.string().min(120).max(160),
  ogImage: z.string().optional(), // path relative to /public, or URL
  noIndex: z.boolean().default(false),
});

const contentStatus = z.enum(["draft", "published"]);

// ── Career Collection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Enforces the two-layer architecture. Permanent fields never change after
// publish. Living fields are updated annually (lastReviewed is the signal).

const careerCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: ({ image }) =>
    z.object({
      // ── Identity ──
      company: z.string(),               // "BSH Hausgeräte GmbH"
      companyShort: z.string(),          // "BSH / Siemens" — for cards
      slug: z.string(),                  // "bsh" — matches filename
      location: z.string(),              // "Munich, Germany"
      period: z.object({
        start: z.string(),               // "2020-05"
        end: z.string().or(z.literal("present")), // "2024-11"
      }),
      logo: image().optional(),          // Company logo (processed by Sharp)

      // ── PERMANENT LAYER (never edit after publish) ──
      role: z.string(),                  // "Senior Brand Communications Manager (Global)"
      roleHistory: z
        .array(
          z.object({
            title: z.string(),
            period: z.string(),
          })
        )
        .optional(),                     // For multi-role tenures like BSH

      wins: z.array(
        z.object({
          headline: z.string(),          // "€1.62M organic media value via purchase-intent content"
          metric: z.string().optional(), // "€1.62M" — for structured display
          context: z.string().optional(), // full sentence with context
        })
      ),                                 // Used to generate WorkExperience schema

      skills: z.array(z.string()),       // ["B2B Marketing", "Brand Strategy", "Agile"]
      tools: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // ["Jira", "Adobe Suite", "TikTok Ads"]
      departureContext: z.string(),      // Honest, professional. Always present.

      // ── LIVING LAYER (updated annually, dated) ──
      currentRelevance: z.object({
        date: z.string(),                // ISO date of last update "2026-06"
        body: z.string(),                // Markdown: "In 2026, this experience is relevant because..."
        audienceAngles: z
          .object({
            recruiter: z.string().optional(),
            customer: z.string().optional(),
            collaborator: z.string().optional(),
          })
          .optional(),
      }),

      projectsFromHere: z
        .array(
          z.object({
            title: z.string(),           // "vision-intelligence"
            url: z.string(),             // "/projects/vision-intelligence"
            connection: z.string(),      // "The gap I saw at BSH became this product."
          })
        )
        .optional(),

      lastReviewed: z.string(),          // ISO date "2026-06-13" — drives the "freshness" signal

      // ── SEO ──
      seo: seoFields,
      status: contentStatus.default("draft"),
      order: z.number().optional(),     // For manual ordering on the index page
    }),
});

// ── Music Collection ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// MusicRecording-ready. Every field maps directly to a schema.org property.

const musicCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: ({ image }) =>
    z.object({
      title: z.string(),
      slug: z.string(),
      artwork: image().optional(),

      // ── MusicRecording schema fields ──
      byArtist: z.string().default("Berk Saraloglu"),
      duration: z.string().optional(),   // ISO 8601: "PT3M42S"
      genre: z.array(z.string()),        // ["Indie Pop", "Singer-Songwriter"]
      datePublished: z.string().optional(), // ISO date
      isrc: z.string().optional(),       // International Standard Recording Code

      // ── Streaming links ──
      spotify: z.string().url().optional(),
      appleMusic: z.string().url().optional(),
      youtube: z.string().url().optional(),
      soundcloud: z.string().url().optional(),

      // ── Song archaeology (the moat) ──
      releaseStatus: z.enum(["released", "demo", "writing", "archived"]),
      writingProcess: z.string().optional(), // How the song started
      behindTheLyrics: z.string().optional(), // What it's actually about
      productionNotes: z.string().optional(), // Instrumentation, recording approach
      influences: z.array(z.string()).optional(),

      // ── SEO ──
      seo: seoFields,
      status: contentStatus.default("draft"),
    }),
});

// ── Projects Collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Non-technical pitch pages. SoftwareApplication schema fields included.

const projectsCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: ({ image }) =>
    z.object({
      title: z.string(),
      slug: z.string(),
      tagline: z.string().max(100),      // One-sentence pitch
      cover: image().optional(),

      // ── Project status (distinct from contentStatus) ──
      projectStatus: z.enum(["concept", "mvp", "beta", "live", "archived"]),

      // ── Non-technical pitch fields ──
      problem: z.string(),               // The problem it solves (1 sentence)
      audience: z.string(),              // Who it's for
      howItWorks: z.string(),            // Plain-language explanation
      techStack: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // For technical visitors

      // ── Links ──
      githubUrl: z.string().url().optional(),
      liveUrl: z.string().url().optional(),
      waitlistUrl: z.string().url().optional(),

      // ── SoftwareApplication schema fields ──
      applicationCategory: z.string().optional(), // "BusinessApplication"
      operatingSystem: z.string().optional(),      // "Web"
      offers: z
        .object({
          price: z.string(),             // "0" for free, or price
          priceCurrency: z.string().default("EUR"),
        })
        .optional(),

      // ── Career connection (back-links to living layer) ──
      originStory: z.string().optional(), // "This idea started at BSH when..."
      careerOrigin: z.string().optional(), // slug of career page that seeded this

      // ── SEO ──
      seo: seoFields,
      status: contentStatus.default("draft"),
      featured: z.boolean().default(false),
      order: z.number().optional(),
    }),
});

// ── Trips Collection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Single collection with a level discriminator — avoids schema duplication.

const tripsCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: ({ image }) =>
    z.object({
      title: z.string(),                 // "Japan" or "Tokyo"
      slug: z.string(),                  // "japan" or "japan/tokyo"
      level: z.enum(["country", "city"]),
      parentSlug: z.string().optional(), // "japan" for city-level entries
      cover: image().optional(),
      visitedAt: z.string(),             // "2023-11" or "2023-11-15"
      visitCount: z.number().default(1),

      // ── Content ──
      highlight: z.string(),             // 1-sentence hook (used on index)
      recommendation: z.string().optional(), // Genuine rec, not TripAdvisor

      // ── SEO ──
      seo: seoFields,
      status: contentStatus.default("draft"),
    }),
});

// ── Writing Collection (The Intersection essays) ──────────────────────────────
// The `intersects` field enforces the cross-domain format at schema level.

const writingCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: () =>
    z.object({
      title: z.string(),
      slug: z.string(),
      publishedAt: z.string(),           // ISO date
      updatedAt: z.string().optional(),

      // ── Intersection format ──
      intersects: z
        .array(
          z.enum([
            "marketing",
            "music",
            "ai",
            "product",
            "career",
            "design",
            "culture",
          ])
        )
        .min(2),                         // Enforces cross-domain at schema level

      // ── Content metadata ──
      excerpt: z.string().max(280),      // Twitter-card length summary
      readingTime: z.number().optional(), // minutes

      // ── SEO ──
      seo: seoFields,
      status: contentStatus.default("draft"),
      featured: z.boolean().default(false),
    }),
});

// ── Export ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const collections = {
  career: careerCollection,
  music: musicCollection,
  projects: projectsCollection,
  trips: tripsCollection,
  writing: writingCollection,
};
```

---

## 3. Dynamic Route Map

| URL Pattern | Astro File | Collection | getStaticPaths filter |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/career` | `src/pages/career/index.astro` | `career` | `status: published`, sorted by `period.end` desc |
| `/career/[slug]` | `src/pages/career/[slug].astro` | `career` | `status: published` |
| `/music` | `src/pages/music/index.astro` | `music` | `status: published`, sorted by `datePublished` desc |
| `/music/[slug]` | `src/pages/music/[slug].astro` | `music` | `status: published` |
| `/projects` | `src/pages/projects/index.astro` | `projects` | `status: published`, sorted by `order` then `featured` |
| `/projects/[slug]` | `src/pages/projects/[slug].astro` | `projects` | `status: published` |
| `/trips` | `src/pages/trips/index.astro` | `trips` | `status: published`, `level: country` |
| `/trips/[slug]` | `src/pages/trips/[slug].astro` | `trips` | `status: published` |
| `/writing` | `src/pages/writing/index.astro` | `writing` | `status: published`, sorted by `publishedAt` desc |
| `/writing/[slug]` | `src/pages/writing/[slug].astro` | `writing` | `status: published` |
| `/ai-marketing-dach` | `src/pages/ai-marketing-dach.astro` | — | Static page (no collection) |
| `/about` | `src/pages/about.astro` | — | Static page |
| `/work` | `src/pages/work.astro` | — | Static page |
| `/now` | `src/pages/now.astro` | — | Static page (update manually monthly) |

**Slug convention:** always lowercase, hyphen-separated, matches the MDX filename. No underscores.

---

## 4. Shared TypeScript Types (`src/types/index.ts`)

```typescript
// Content status — used across all collections
export type ContentStatus = "draft" | "published";

// Audience types — used for intent routing and SEO targeting
export type AudienceType = "recruiter" | "customer" | "collaborator" | "fan";

// Topical cluster tags — used for internal linking
export type ClusterTag =
  | "ai-marketing-dach"
  | "indie-songwriter"
  | "expat-career-germany";

// Domain intersection tags — used in writing collection
export type DomainTag =
  | "marketing"
  | "music"
  | "ai"
  | "product"
  | "career"
  | "design"
  | "culture";

// SEO fields shared across page types
export interface SEOFields {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  ogImage?: string;
  noIndex?: boolean;
}

// JSON-LD base type
export interface JsonLdBase {
  "@context": "https://schema.org";
  "@type": string;
}

// Project status for UI badges
export type ProjectStatus = "concept" | "mvp" | "beta" | "live" | "archived";

// Music release status
export type MusicReleaseStatus = "released" | "demo" | "writing" | "archived";
```

---

## 5. Subdomain Plan

| Subdomain | Status | Action Now | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `saralogy.com` | Active | Configure DNS → Vercel | Main site |
| `www.saralogy.com` | Active (redirect) | Vercel redirects www → apex | SEO canonical |
| `app.saralogy.com` | Parked | CNAME → Vercel (no project) | AI service product (vision-intelligence) — activate when ready |
| `api.saralogy.com` | Parked | CNAME → Vercel (no project) | API for AI service — activate when ready |
| `blog.saralogy.com` | **Deferred** | Do NOT configure yet | Would split SEO authority from main domain. Writing lives at `/writing`, not on a subdomain. |

**Why blog subdomain is explicitly deferred:** Every page on a subdomain builds authority on the subdomain, not on `saralogy.com`. For a site in early authority-building phase, splitting authority is a net negative. The `/writing` section on the main domain compounds authority. Revisit at 24 months if the site needs dedicated CMS infrastructure.

---

## 6. Architecture Decisions Log

| # | Decision | Rationale | Alternative Rejected |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Content collections over CMS** | ≤3h/week content cadence; no team; Astro collections are type-safe and git-versioned | Contentful / Sanity: adds API latency, cost, external dependency for a one-person operation |
| 2 | **MDX over MD** | Allows embedding React/Astro components inside content (song player, interactive maps) without moving to a different system | Plain MD: can't embed interactive components. Full Astro: requires a page file per content entry (no bulk authoring) |
| 3 | **Single `trips` collection with `level` discriminator** | Avoids schema duplication for country vs city entries; `parentSlug` enables tree traversal | Two collections (`trips-country`, `trips-city`): redundant schemas, more config, harder to query together |
| 4 | **`intersects: z.array(...).min(2)` on writing** | Schema-level enforcement of "The Intersection" format — makes it impossible to publish an essay without naming two domains | No enforcement: format degrades over time without the constraint |
| 5 | **`wins` as structured array in career** | Enables programmatic generation of WorkExperience schema from structured data, not raw prose | Free text only: loses the machine-readable structure needed for AEO |
| 6 | **Two-layer architecture as schema contract** | `currentRelevance` and `lastReviewed` are required fields, not optional. Forces the living layer to be present at publish. | Optional fields: authors skip the living layer, pages decay |
| 7 | **`seoFields` sub-schema on every collection** | Centralizes SEO validation; enforces title ≤60 chars and description 120–160 chars at content authoring time | Ad-hoc meta on each collection: drifts, inconsistent limits, validation only at render time |
| 8 | **`/lib/schema/` as separate module** | JSON-LD builders are TypeScript functions, not inline strings. Type-safe, testable, reusable across page types | Inline JSON-LD in each layout: duplicated, drifts, can't be unit tested |
| 9 | **`src/pages/ai-marketing-dach.astro` as static page** | Pillar page needs hand-crafted structure, schema markup, and internal links — not suitable for a generic collection template | Putting it in a `writing` collection: loses flexibility, forces it into the essay template |
| 10 | **Path aliases in tsconfig** | `@components/`, `@layouts/`, `@lib/` import aliases prevent `../../../../` chains | Relative imports: fragile, hard to refactor when moving files |
| 11 | **`artwork` and `cover` use Astro `image()` schema** | Enables Sharp-based optimization at build time; generates responsive srcsets automatically | Storing as URLs: bypasses optimization, increases page weight |
| 12 | **`status: draft` as default** | Nothing publishes accidentally; explicit intent required to go live | `status: published` as default: risk of partial drafts appearing in production |
| 13 | **`src/lib/schema/` not `src/components/seo/`** | JSON-LD builders are pure TypeScript functions (data → string), not UI components. Separating concerns: data in lib, rendering in components | Putting JSON-LD builders in components: mixes data construction with rendering logic |
| 14 | **`blog.saralogy.com` subdomain deferred** | SEO authority concentration on main domain is the priority for the first 24 months. Subdomain = authority split | Subdomain from day one: wastes early link equity by splitting it across domains |

---

## 7. `tsconfig.json` (with path aliases)

```json
{
  "extends": "astro/tsconfigs/strict",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths": {
      "@components/*": ["src/components/*"],
      "@layouts/*": ["src/layouts/*"],
      "@lib/*": ["src/lib/*"],
      "@types/*": ["src/types/*"],
      "@assets/*": ["src/assets/*"]
    },
    "strictNullChecks": true,
    "noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true
  }
}
```

---

## 8. Implementation Sequence (10 steps, each independently deployable)

1. **Scaffold** — `bunx create astro@latest saralogy` (use the Empty template), install Tailwind + sitemap, commit `astro.config.mjs`, `vercel.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
2. **Base layout** — `Base.astro`, `global.css`, `Header.astro`, `Footer.astro`; deploy to Vercel and verify CI passes
3. **Content collections** — `src/content/config.ts` with all 5 schemas; create stub MDX files for each collection type
4. **Homepage** — `index.astro` with intent router and basic sections; confirm routing works
5. **About page** — `about.astro` with Person schema, FAQ block; this is MVP page #1
6. **Career section** — `[slug].astro` template + `bsh.mdx` content; two-layer architecture live; this is MVP page #2
7. **Pillar page** — `ai-marketing-dach.astro` with full content and internal links; this is MVP page #3
8. **Music section** — `music/index.astro` + `[slug].astro` + first 2 song pages
9. **Projects section** — `projects/index.astro` + `[slug].astro` + vision-intelligence + resume-matcher
10. **Launch prep** — `/now`, `/work`, sitemap submission to Search Console, analytics

# A1 Systems Architecture — saralogy.com

**Agent:** A1 (Systems Architect) | **Date:** 2026-06-13

## 2. Content Collection Zod Schemas (`src/content/config.ts`)

```typescript
import { defineCollection, z } from "astro:content";

// ── Shared sub-schemas ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

const seoFields = z.object({
  title: z.string().max(60),
  description: z.string().min(120).max(160),
  ogImage: z.string().optional(), // path relative to /public, or URL
  noIndex: z.boolean().default(false),
});

const contentStatus = z.enum(["draft", "published"]);

// ── Career Collection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Enforces the two-layer architecture. Permanent fields never change after
// publish. Living fields are updated annually (lastReviewed is the signal).

const careerCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: ({ image }) =>
    z.object({
      // ── Identity ──
      company: z.string(),               // "BSH Hausgeräte GmbH"
      companyShort: z.string(),          // "BSH / Siemens" — for cards
      slug: z.string(),                  // "bsh" — matches filename
      location: z.string(),              // "Munich, Germany"
      period: z.object({
        start: z.string(),               // "2020-05"
        end: z.string().or(z.literal("present")), // "2024-11"
      }),
      logo: image().optional(),          // Company logo (processed by Sharp)

      // ── PERMANENT LAYER (never edit after publish) ──
      role: z.string(),                  // "Senior Brand Communications Manager (Global)"
      roleHistory: z
        .array(
          z.object({
            title: z.string(),
            period: z.string(),
          })
        )
        .optional(),                     // For multi-role tenures like BSH

      wins: z.array(
        z.object({
          headline: z.string(),          // "€1.62M organic media value via purchase-intent content"
          metric: z.string().optional(), // "€1.62M" — for structured display
          context: z.string().optional(), // full sentence with context
        })
      ),                                 // Used to generate WorkExperience schema

      skills: z.array(z.string()),       // ["B2B Marketing", "Brand Strategy", "Agile"]
      tools: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // ["Jira", "Adobe Suite", "TikTok Ads"]
      departureContext: z.string(),      // Honest, professional. Always present.

      // ── LIVING LAYER (updated annually, dated) ──
      currentRelevance: z.object({
        date: z.string(),                // ISO date of last update "2026-06"
        body: z.string(),                // Markdown: "In 2026, this experience is relevant because..."
        audienceAngles: z
          .object({
            recruiter: z.string().optional(),
            customer: z.string().optional(),
            collaborator: z.string().optional(),
          })
          .optional(),
      }),

      projectsFromHere: z
        .array(
          z.object({
            title: z.string(),           // "vision-intelligence"
            url: z.string(),             // "/projects/vision-intelligence"
            connection: z.string(),      // "The gap I saw at BSH became this product."
          })
        )
        .optional(),

      lastReviewed: z.string(),          // ISO date "2026-06-13" — drives the "freshness" signal

      // ── SEO ──
      seo: seoFields,
      status: contentStatus.default("draft"),
      order: z.number().optional(),     // For manual ordering on the index page
    }),
});

// ── Music Collection ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// MusicRecording-ready. Every field maps directly to a schema.org property.

const musicCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: ({ image }) =>
    z.object({
      title: z.string(),
      slug: z.string(),
      artwork: image().optional(),

      // ── MusicRecording schema fields ──
      byArtist: z.string().default("Berk Saraloglu"),
      duration: z.string().optional(),   // ISO 8601: "PT3M42S"
      genre: z.array(z.string()),        // ["Indie Pop", "Singer-Songwriter"]
      datePublished: z.string().optional(), // ISO date
      isrc: z.string().optional(),       // International Standard Recording Code

      // ── Streaming links ──
      spotify: z.string().url().optional(),
      appleMusic: z.string().url().optional(),
      youtube: z.string().url().optional(),
      soundcloud: z.string().url().optional(),

      // ── Song archaeology (the moat) ──
      releaseStatus: z.enum(["released", "demo", "writing", "archived"]),
      writingProcess: z.string().optional(), // How the song started
      behindTheLyrics: z.string().optional(), // What it's actually about
      productionNotes: z.string().optional(), // Instrumentation, recording approach
      influences: z.array(z.string()).optional(),

      // ── SEO ──
      seo: seoFields,
      status: contentStatus.default("draft"),
    }),
});

// ── Projects Collection ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Non-technical pitch pages. SoftwareApplication schema fields included.

const projectsCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: ({ image }) =>
    z.object({
      title: z.string(),
      slug: z.string(),
      tagline: z.string().max(100),      // One-sentence pitch
      cover: image().optional(),

      // ── Project status (distinct from contentStatus) ──
      projectStatus: z.enum(["concept", "mvp", "beta", "live", "archived"]),

      // ── Non-technical pitch fields ──
      problem: z.string(),               // The problem it solves (1 sentence)
      audience: z.string(),              // Who it's for
      howItWorks: z.string(),            // Plain-language explanation
      techStack: z.array(z.string()).optional(), // For technical visitors

      // ── Links ──
      githubUrl: z.string().url().optional(),
      liveUrl: z.string().url().optional(),
      waitlistUrl: z.string().url().optional(),

      // ── SoftwareApplication schema fields ──
      applicationCategory: z.string().optional(), // "BusinessApplication"
      operatingSystem: z.string().optional(),      // "Web"
      offers: z
        .object({
          price: z.string(),             // "0" for free, or price
          priceCurrency: z.string().default("EUR"),
        })
        .optional(),

      // ── Career connection (back-links to living layer) ──
      originStory: z.string().optional(), // "This idea started at BSH when..."
      careerOrigin: z.string().optional(), // slug of career page that seeded this

      // ── SEO ──
      seo: seoFields,
      status: contentStatus.default("draft"),
      featured: z.boolean().default(false),
      order: z.number().optional(),
    }),
});

// ── Trips Collection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Single collection with a level discriminator — avoids schema duplication.

const tripsCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: ({ image }) =>
    z.object({
      title: z.string(),                 // "Japan" or "Tokyo"
      slug: z.string(),                  // "japan" or "japan/tokyo"
      level: z.enum(["country", "city"]),
      parentSlug: z.string().optional(), // "japan" for city-level entries
      cover: image().optional(),
      visitedAt: z.string(),             // "2023-11" or "2023-11-15"
      visitCount: z.number().default(1),

      // ── Content ──
      highlight: z.string(),             // 1-sentence hook (used on index)
      recommendation: z.string().optional(), // Genuine rec, not TripAdvisor

      // ── SEO ──
      seo: seoFields,
      status: contentStatus.default("draft"),
    }),
});

// ── Writing Collection (The Intersection essays) ──────────────────────────────
// The `intersects` field enforces the cross-domain format at schema level.

const writingCollection = defineCollection({
  type: "content",
  schema: () =>
    z.object({
      title: z.string(),
      slug: z.string(),
      publishedAt: z.string(),           // ISO date
      updatedAt: z.string().optional(),

      // ── Intersection format ──
      intersects: z
        .array(
          z.enum([
            "marketing",
            "music",
            "ai",
            "product",
            "career",
            "design",
            "culture",
          ])
        )
        .min(2),                         // Enforces cross-domain at schema level

      // ── Content metadata ──
      excerpt: z.string().max(280),      // Twitter-card length summary
      readingTime: z.number().optional(), // minutes

      // ── SEO ──
      seo: seoFields,
      status: contentStatus.default("draft"),
      featured: z.boolean().default(false),
    }),
});

// ── Export ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export const collections = {
  career: careerCollection,
  music: musicCollection,
  projects: projectsCollection,
  trips: tripsCollection,
  writing: writingCollection,
};
```

---

## 3. Dynamic Route Map

| URL Pattern | Astro File | Collection | getStaticPaths filter |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/career` | `src/pages/career/index.astro` | `career` | `status: published`, sorted by `period.end` desc |
| `/career/[slug]` | `src/pages/career/[slug].astro` | `career` | `status: published` |
| `/music` | `src/pages/music/index.astro` | `music` | `status: published`, sorted by `datePublished` desc |
| `/music/[slug]` | `src/pages/music/[slug].astro` | `music` | `status: published` |
| `/projects` | `src/pages/projects/index.astro` | `projects` | `status: published`, sorted by `order` then `featured` |
| `/projects/[slug]` | `src/pages/projects/[slug].astro` | `projects` | `status: published` |
| `/trips` | `src/pages/trips/index.astro` | `trips` | `status: published`, `level: country` |
| `/trips/[slug]` | `src/pages/trips/[slug].astro` | `trips` | `status: published` |
| `/writing` | `src/pages/writing/index.astro` | `writing` | `status: published`, sorted by `publishedAt` desc |
| `/writing/[slug]` | `src/pages/writing/[slug].astro` | `writing` | `status: published` |
| `/ai-marketing-dach` | `src/pages/ai-marketing-dach.astro` | — | Static page (no collection) |
| `/about` | `src/pages/about.astro` | — | Static page |
| `/work` | `src/pages/work.astro` | — | Static page |
| `/now` | `src/pages/now.astro` | — | Static page (update manually monthly) |

**Slug convention:** always lowercase, hyphen-separated, matches the MDX filename. No underscores.

---

## 4. Shared TypeScript Types (`src/types/index.ts`)

```typescript
// Content status — used across all collections
export type ContentStatus = "draft" | "published";

// Audience types — used for intent routing and SEO targeting
export type AudienceType = "recruiter" | "customer" | "collaborator" | "fan";

// Topical cluster tags — used for internal linking
export type ClusterTag =
  | "ai-marketing-dach"
  | "indie-songwriter"
  | "expat-career-germany";

// Domain intersection tags — used in writing collection
export type DomainTag =
  | "marketing"
  | "music"
  | "ai"
  | "product"
  | "career"
  | "design"
  | "culture";

// SEO fields shared across page types
export interface SEOFields {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  ogImage?: string;
  noIndex?: boolean;
}

// JSON-LD base type
export interface JsonLdBase {
  "@context": "https://schema.org";
  "@type": string;
}

// Project status for UI badges
export type ProjectStatus = "concept" | "mvp" | "beta" | "live" | "archived";

// Music release status
export type MusicReleaseStatus = "released" | "demo" | "writing" | "archived";
```

---

## 5. Subdomain Plan

| Subdomain | Status | Action Now | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `saralogy.com` | Active | Configure DNS → Vercel | Main site |
| `www.saralogy.com` | Active (redirect) | Vercel redirects www → apex | SEO canonical |
| `app.saralogy.com` | Parked | CNAME → Vercel (no project) | AI service product (vision-intelligence) — activate when ready |
| `api.saralogy.com` | Parked | CNAME → Vercel (no project) | API for AI service — activate when ready |
| `blog.saralogy.com` | **Deferred** | Do NOT configure yet | Would split SEO authority from main domain. Writing lives at `/writing`, not on a subdomain. |

**Why blog subdomain is explicitly deferred:** Every page on a subdomain builds authority on the subdomain, not on `saralogy.com`. For a site in early authority-building phase, splitting authority is a net negative. The `/writing` section on the main domain compounds authority. Revisit at 24 months if the site needs dedicated CMS infrastructure.

---

## 6. Architecture Decisions Log

| # | Decision | Rationale | Alternative Rejected |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Content collections over CMS** | ≤3h/week content cadence; no team; Astro collections are type-safe and git-versioned | Contentful / Sanity: adds API latency, cost, external dependency for a one-person operation |
| 2 | **MDX over MD** | Allows embedding React/Astro components inside content (song player, interactive maps) without moving to a different system | Plain MD: can't embed interactive components. Full Astro: requires a page file per content entry (no bulk authoring) |
| 3 | **Single `trips` collection with `level` discriminator** | Avoids schema duplication for country vs city entries; `parentSlug` enables tree traversal | Two collections (`trips-country`, `trips-city`): redundant schemas, more config, harder to query together |
| 4 | **`intersects: z.array(...).min(2)` on writing** | Schema-level enforcement of "The Intersection" format — makes it impossible to publish an essay without naming two domains | No enforcement: format degrades over time without the constraint |
| 5 | **`wins` as structured array in career** | Enables programmatic generation of WorkExperience schema from structured data, not raw prose | Free text only: loses the machine-readable structure needed for AEO |
| 6 | **Two-layer architecture as schema contract** | `currentRelevance` and `lastReviewed` are required fields, not optional. Forces the living layer to be present at publish. | Optional fields: authors skip the living layer, pages decay |
| 7 | **`seoFields` sub-schema on every collection** | Centralizes SEO validation; enforces title ≤60 chars and description 120–160 chars at content authoring time | Ad-hoc meta on each collection: drifts, inconsistent limits, validation only at render time |
| 8 | **`/lib/schema/` as separate module** | JSON-LD builders are TypeScript functions, not inline strings. Type-safe, testable, reusable across page types | Inline JSON-LD in each layout: duplicated, drifts, can't be unit tested |
| 9 | **`src/pages/ai-marketing-dach.astro` as static page** | Pillar page needs hand-crafted structure, schema markup, and internal links — not suitable for a generic collection template | Putting it in a `writing` collection: loses flexibility, forces it into the essay template |
| 10 | **Path aliases in tsconfig** | `@components/`, `@layouts/`, `@lib/` import aliases prevent `../../../../` chains | Relative imports: fragile, hard to refactor when moving files |
| 11 | **`artwork` and `cover` use Astro `image()` schema** | Enables Sharp-based optimization at build time; generates responsive srcsets automatically | Storing as URLs: bypasses optimization, increases page weight |
| 12 | **`status: draft` as default** | Nothing publishes accidentally; explicit intent required to go live | `status: published` as default: risk of partial drafts appearing in production |
| 13 | **`src/lib/schema/` not `src/components/seo/`** | JSON-LD builders are pure TypeScript functions (data → string), not UI components. Separating concerns: data in lib, rendering in components | Putting JSON-LD builders in components: mixes data construction with rendering logic |
| 14 | **`blog.saralogy.com` subdomain deferred** | SEO authority concentration on main domain is the priority for the first 24 months. Subdomain = authority split | Subdomain from day one: wastes early link equity by splitting it across domains |

---

## 7. `tsconfig.json` (with path aliases)

```json
{
  "extends": "astro/tsconfigs/strict",

## 8. Implementation Sequence (10 steps, each independently deployable)

1. **Scaffold** — `bunx create astro@latest saralogy` (use the Empty template), install Tailwind + sitemap, commit `astro.config.mjs`, `vercel.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
2. **Base layout** — `Base.astro`, `global.css`, `Header.astro`, `Footer.astro`; deploy to Vercel and verify CI passes
3. **Content collections** — `src/content/config.ts` with all 5 schemas; create stub MDX files for each collection type
4. **Homepage** — `index.astro` with intent router and basic sections; confirm routing works
5. **About page** — `about.astro` with Person schema, FAQ block; this is MVP page #1
6. **Career section** — `[slug].astro` template + `bsh.mdx` content; two-layer architecture live; this is MVP page #2
7. **Pillar page** — `ai-marketing-dach.astro` with full content and internal links; this is MVP page #3
8. **Music section** — `music/index.astro` + `[slug].astro` + first 2 song pages
9. **Projects section** — `projects/index.astro` + `[slug].astro` + vision-intelligence + resume-matcher
10. **Launch prep** — `/now`, `/work`, sitemap submission to Search Console, analytics

# A2 SEO + AEO Spec — saralogy.com

**Agent:** A2 (SEO Architect) | **Date:** 2026-06-13
**Stack:** Astro 4+ · schema.org · JSON-LD · Vercel

---

## 1. Technical SEO Foundations

### robots.txt

Astro with `@astrojs/sitemap` auto-generates `/robots.txt` if configured. Override with a manual file in `/public/robots.txt`:

```
User-agent: *
Allow: /

# Block draft pages from indexing
Disallow: /draft/
Disallow: /_astro/

# Sitemap location — submit this URL to Google Search Console
Sitemap: https://saralogy.com/sitemap-index.xml
```

**Implementation:** Place in `public/robots.txt`. Astro will serve it at `saralogy.com/robots.txt` automatically.

### Sitemap Strategy

Use `@astrojs/sitemap` (already in `astro.config.mjs`). It auto-generates from all static routes.

**What to include:**
- All `/career/[slug]` pages (published only — filter via `status: published` in collection)
- All `/music/[slug]` pages (published + released status)
- All `/projects/[slug]` pages (published only)
- All `/writing/[slug]` pages (published only)
- Static pages: `/`, `/about`, `/work`, `/career`, `/music`, `/projects`, `/ai-marketing-dach`

**What to exclude:**
- `/404` — handled by `filter: (page) => !page.includes("/404")`
- `/draft/*` — handled by `filter: (page) => !page.includes("/draft/")`
- `/now` — consider including (freshness signal) or excluding (thin content). **Decision: include** — the monthly update is real content, not boilerplate.

**`changefreq` per section:**
- Homepage, /about, /work: `monthly`
- /career/* pages: `yearly` (living layer updates annually)
- /music/* pages: `yearly`
- /projects/*: `monthly` (status changes)
- /ai-marketing-dach (pillar): `monthly`
- /now: `monthly`
- /writing/*: `yearly` (evergreen)

### Canonical Tag Rules

Set `<link rel="canonical">` on every page. Rules:
1. **Self-referencing canonical** — every page's canonical = its own URL. No exceptions for saralogy.com (single domain, no content syndication initially).
2. **www vs non-www** — canonical always points to `https://saralogy.com/path` (non-www). The `vercel.json` www redirect handles this at the HTTP level; the canonical tag is belt-and-suspenders.
3. **No trailing slash** — canonical URLs never end with `/` (except the homepage: `https://saralogy.com/`).
4. **Pagination** — not applicable at launch (no paginated lists). If added later, use `rel="next"` / `rel="prev"`.

**Astro implementation** in `Base.astro`:
```astro
---
const canonicalURL = new URL(Astro.url.pathname, Astro.site);
---
<link rel="canonical" href={canonicalURL} />
```

### hreflang Decision

**Decision: DEFER.** Do not implement hreflang at launch.

**Rationale:** The site launches in English. The DACH market and expat career cluster could benefit from German versions of `/ai-marketing-dach` and `/career` pages — but partial localization (2 pages in German, 20 in English) creates more SEO confusion than it resolves. Implement hreflang when a full German-language version of a section is ready (at minimum 5 pages in a cluster).

**When to revisit:** After landing the Senior Marketing Manager role and having steady-state time, translate the AI marketing DACH cluster into German.

### 404 Handling

Create `src/pages/404.astro`. Vercel serves it automatically for unmatched routes (configured in `vercel.json`).

The 404 page should:
- Show top navigation (so users can find their way)
- Suggest 3 relevant pages (About, Career, Music)
- Not include a search form (overkill for a personal site)
- Include the full `<head>` with `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">` to prevent 404 pages from being indexed

---

## 2. Core Web Vitals Targets

### LCP (Largest Contentful Paint)
**Target:** ≤2.5s (Good)

| Risk | Astro-specific fix |
|---|---|
| Hero image not preloaded | Add `<link rel="preload" as="image">` for the above-the-fold image in `Base.astro` |
| Web fonts blocking render | Use `font-display: swap` in CSS; preload font files with `<link rel="preload" as="font">` |
| Large hero image | Use Astro's `<Image />` component — generates WebP + responsive srcset automatically |

**The one thing that most commonly kills LCP on Astro sites:** Importing a React/Svelte component with `client:load` that includes a large image. The hydration delay pushes LCP beyond 2.5s. Use `client:visible` or `client:idle` instead, or keep images outside interactive components.

### CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift)
**Target:** ≤0.1 (Good)

| Risk | Fix |
|---|---|
| Images without explicit dimensions | Always set `width` and `height` (or use `<Image />` which sets them automatically) |
| Web fonts causing FOUT | `font-display: swap` prevents invisible text; use system font fallback with similar metrics |
| Late-injected content (ads, embeds) | Reserve space for YouTube/Spotify embeds with a fixed aspect-ratio wrapper: `aspect-video` |

**The one thing:** Not setting `width`/`height` on images. Astro's `<Image />` component sets these automatically from the source image — use it for all `src/assets/` images.

### INP (Interaction to Next Paint, replaces FID)
**Target:** ≤200ms (Good)

**For a static Astro site with zero client-side JS by default:** INP is not a concern unless you add interactive components. If you add a client-side component (e.g., a music player, a contact form), use `client:idle` or `client:visible` to defer hydration.

**The one thing:** Using `client:load` on a component that isn't needed immediately. Default to `client:visible` for all interactive components.

## 7. AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) Checklist

Steps to maximize probability of being cited by ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews.

- [ ] **Entity disambiguation** — `/about` page is the canonical entity page. All other pages reference `@id: "https://saralogy.com/#person"` in their JSON-LD. This creates a consistent entity graph.
- [ ] **Person schema `sameAs`** — link to all profile pages where Berk's name appears: LinkedIn, GitHub, Spotify, Apple Music. Each `sameAs` URL is a citation anchor for LLMs.
- [ ] **FAQ blocks on every pillar page** — use `FAQPage` schema. Questions should mirror exact phrasing of common queries ("What is AI marketing for SMEs?", "How do I implement AI marketing in Germany?").
- [ ] **FAQ on `/about`** — 4 questions (Who is, What does, Where is, What experience). These are the exact entity-resolution questions LLMs receive.
- [ ] **Complete sentences in meta descriptions** — LLMs often extract meta descriptions for entity summaries. Write them as complete, standalone statements, not keyword lists.
- [ ] **`description` on all JSON-LD** — the `description` field in Person schema should be 2-3 complete sentences that would stand alone as a biographical summary.
- [ ] **Consistent NAP across all pages:**
  - Name: always "Berk Saraloglu" (never "Berk S." or just "Berk")
  - Handle: @berksaraloglu
  - Location: always "Hamburg, Germany"
- [ ] **`knowsAbout` array completeness** — list all topics where Berk has genuine expertise. LLMs use this to route "who knows about X?" queries.
- [ ] **Outbound links to authoritative sources** — link to Wikipedia/authoritative pages when citing industry concepts. This signals to search engines (and by extension LLMs) that the content is well-grounded.
- [ ] **Date freshness** — `dateModified` in Article/BlogPosting schema must be updated when living layer content is edited. Stale `dateModified` reduces citation probability for time-sensitive queries.

## 8. SEO Anti-Patterns to Avoid on Astro Personal Sites

**1. Dynamic routes that don't filter drafts**
If `getStaticPaths()` doesn't filter `status === "published"`, draft MDX files generate real URLs that get indexed. Fix: always filter in `getStaticPaths`.

```typescript
// ❌ Wrong
const entries = await getCollection("career");

// ✅ Correct
const entries = await getCollection("career", ({ data }) => data.status === "published");
```

**2. Missing `site` in `astro.config.mjs`**
Without `site: "https://saralogy.com"`, the sitemap integration generates relative URLs, canonical tags fail, and OG URLs are relative. This is already set in A3's config — do not remove it.

**3. Importing images from `/public` with `<Image />`**
Astro's `<Image />` component cannot process images from `/public`. Use it only for images in `src/assets/`. For `/public/og-default.png`, use a regular `<img>` tag or a direct URL string.

**4. Over-using `client:load` on interactive components**
Every `client:load` directive ships JavaScript to the browser and blocks INP. For a personal site, almost nothing needs `client:load`. Use `client:visible` for below-the-fold interactivity, `client:idle` for non-critical features. A music player that appears halfway down the page does not need `client:load`.

**5. Not including `lastmod` in sitemap entries**
Google prioritizes crawling pages with recent `lastmod` values. The `@astrojs/sitemap` integration uses `new Date()` as the global `lastmod`, which is correct. For career pages specifically, override `lastmod` with the `lastReviewed` field from the content collection — this accurately signals when the living layer was actually updated.

```typescript
// In astro.config.mjs sitemap customization
serialize(item) {
  // For /career/* pages, use the lastReviewed date from frontmatter
  // (requires passing the date through the page's props to the sitemap)
  return item;
}
```

### Canonical Tag Rules

Set `<link rel="canonical">` on every page. Rules:
1. **Self-referencing canonical** — every page's canonical = its own URL. No exceptions for saralogy.com (single domain, no content syndication initially).
2. **www vs non-www** — canonical always points to `https://saralogy.com/path` (non-www). The `vercel.json` www redirect handles this at the HTTP level; the canonical tag is belt-and-suspenders.
3. **No trailing slash** — canonical URLs never end with `/` (except the homepage: `https://saralogy.com/`).
4. **Pagination** — not applicable at launch (no paginated lists). If added later, use `rel="next"` / `rel="prev"`.

**Astro implementation** in `Base.astro`:
```astro
---
const canonicalURL = new URL(Astro.url.pathname, Astro.site);
---
<link rel="canonical" href={canonicalURL} />
```

### hreflang Decision

**Decision: DEFER.** Do not implement hreflang at launch.

**Rationale:** The site launches in English. The DACH market and expat career cluster could benefit from German versions of `/ai-marketing-dach` and `/career` pages — but partial localization (2 pages in German, 20 in English) creates more SEO confusion than it resolves. Implement hreflang when a full German-language version of a section is ready (at minimum 5 pages in a cluster).

**When to revisit:** After landing the Senior Marketing Manager role and having steady-state time, translate the AI marketing DACH cluster into German.

### 404 Handling

Create `src/pages/404.astro`. Vercel serves it automatically for unmatched routes (configured in `vercel.json`).

The 404 page should:
- Show top navigation (so users can find their way)
- Suggest 3 relevant pages (About, Career, Music)
- Not include a search form (overkill for a personal site)
- Include the full `<head>` with `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">` to prevent 404 pages from being indexed

---

## 2. Core Web Vitals Targets

### LCP (Largest Contentful Paint)
**Target:** ≤2.5s (Good)

| Risk | Astro-specific fix |
|---|---|
| Hero image not preloaded | Add `<link rel="preload" as="image">` for the above-the-fold image in `Base.astro` |
| Web fonts blocking render | Use `font-display: swap` in CSS; preload font files with `<link rel="preload" as="font">` |
| Large hero image | Use Astro's `<Image />` component — generates WebP + responsive srcset automatically |

**The one thing that most commonly kills LCP on Astro sites:** Importing a React/Svelte component with `client:load` that includes a large image. The hydration delay pushes LCP beyond 2.5s. Use `client:visible` or `client:idle` instead, or keep images outside interactive components.

### CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift)
**Target:** ≤0.1 (Good)

| Risk | Fix |
|---|---|
| Images without explicit dimensions | Always set `width` and `height` (or use `<Image />` which sets them automatically) |
| Web fonts causing FOUT | `font-display: swap` prevents invisible text; use system font fallback with similar metrics |
| Late-injected content (ads, embeds) | Reserve space for YouTube/Spotify embeds with a fixed aspect-ratio wrapper: `aspect-video` |

**The one thing:** Not setting `width`/`height` on images. Astro's `<Image />` component sets these automatically from the source image — use it for all `src/assets/` images.

### INP (Interaction to Next Paint, replaces FID)
**Target:** ≤200ms (Good)

**For a static Astro site with zero client-side JS by default:** INP is not a concern unless you add interactive components. If you add a client-side component (e.g., a music player, a contact form), use `client:idle` or `client:visible` to defer hydration.

**The one thing:** Using `client:load` on a component that isn't needed immediately. Default to `client:visible` for all interactive components.

## 5. Internal Linking Rules (Topical Authority Triangle)

### The Triangle

```
/career/[slug] ←──────────────────────────────────────────→ /projects/[slug]
     ↑                                                               ↑
     └──────────────────→ /ai-marketing-dach ←────────────────────┘
```

### Mandatory Link Rules

**Every `/career/[slug]` page MUST link to:**
- `/ai-marketing-dach` — anchor: relevant to the marketing + AI cluster
- Any `/projects/[slug]` where `careerOrigin` matches this slug
- `/career` (index) — breadcrumb

**Every `/projects/[slug]` page MUST link to:**
- `/ai-marketing-dach` (if project is AI marketing related)
- `/career/[slug]` listed in `careerOrigin` field
- `/work` — "Want to work with me?" CTA

**Every `/music/[slug]` page MUST link to:**
- `/music` (index) — breadcrumb
- `/music/process` — anchor: "My songwriting process"
- Relevant `/writing/[slug]` if an Intersection essay exists for this song's themes

**Every `/writing/[slug]` (Intersection essay) MUST link to:**
- The `/career/[slug]` pages relevant to the domains in `intersects`
- `/music/[slug]` pages mentioned
- `/projects/[slug]` pages mentioned

**`/ai-marketing-dach` (pillar) MUST link to:**
- All published `/career/[slug]` pages (evidence for the thesis)
- All AI-related `/projects/[slug]` pages
- Relevant `/writing/[slug]` essays

**Anchor text rules:**
- Never use "click here" or "read more"
- Use the target page's primary keyword as anchor text
- Vary anchor text — don't use identical anchors for the same target repeatedly

## 7. AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) Checklist

Steps to maximize probability of being cited by ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews.

- [ ] **Entity disambiguation** — `/about` page is the canonical entity page. All other pages reference `@id: "https://saralogy.com/#person"` in their JSON-LD. This creates a consistent entity graph.
- [ ] **Person schema `sameAs`** — link to all profile pages where Berk's name appears: LinkedIn, GitHub, Spotify, Apple Music. Each `sameAs` URL is a citation anchor for LLMs.
- [ ] **FAQ blocks on every pillar page** — use `FAQPage` schema. Questions should mirror exact phrasing of common queries ("What is AI marketing for SMEs?", "How do I implement AI marketing in Germany?").
- [ ] **FAQ on `/about`** — 4 questions (Who is, What does, Where is, What experience). These are the exact entity-resolution questions LLMs receive.
- [ ] **Complete sentences in meta descriptions** — LLMs often extract meta descriptions for entity summaries. Write them as complete, standalone statements, not keyword lists.
- [ ] **`description` on all JSON-LD** — the `description` field in Person schema should be 2-3 complete sentences that would stand alone as a biographical summary.
- [ ] **Consistent NAP across all pages:**
  - Name: always "Berk Saraloglu" (never "Berk S." or just "Berk")
  - Handle: @berksaraloglu
  - Location: always "Hamburg, Germany"
- [ ] **`knowsAbout` array completeness** — list all topics where Berk has genuine expertise. LLMs use this to route "who knows about X?" queries.
- [ ] **Outbound links to authoritative sources** — link to Wikipedia/authoritative pages when citing industry concepts. This signals to search engines (and by extension LLMs) that the content is well-grounded.
- [ ] **Date freshness** — `dateModified` in Article/BlogPosting schema must be updated when living layer content is edited. Stale `dateModified` reduces citation probability for time-sensitive queries.

---

## 8. SEO Anti-Patterns to Avoid on Astro Personal Sites

**1. Dynamic routes that don't filter drafts**
If `getStaticPaths()` doesn't filter `status === "published"`, draft MDX files generate real URLs that get indexed. Fix: always filter in `getStaticPaths`.

```typescript
// ❌ Wrong
const entries = await getCollection("career");

// ✅ Correct
const entries = await getCollection("career", ({ data }) => data.status === "published");
```

**2. Missing `site` in `astro.config.mjs`**
Without `site: "https://saralogy.com"`, the sitemap integration generates relative URLs, canonical tags fail, and OG URLs are relative. This is already set in A3's config — do not remove it.

**3. Importing images from `/public` with `<Image />`**
Astro's `<Image />` component cannot process images from `/public`. Use it only for images in `src/assets/`. For `/public/og-default.png`, use a regular `<img>` tag or a direct URL string.

**4. Over-using `client:load` on interactive components**
Every `client:load` directive ships JavaScript to the browser and blocks INP. For a personal site, almost nothing needs `client:load`. Use `client:visible` for below-the-fold interactivity, `client:idle` for non-critical features. A music player that appears halfway down the page does not need `client:load`.

**5. Not including `lastmod` in sitemap entries**
Google prioritizes crawling pages with recent `lastmod` values. The `@astrojs/sitemap` integration uses `new Date()` as the global `lastmod`, which is correct. For career pages specifically, override `lastmod` with the `lastReviewed` field from the content collection — this accurately signals when the living layer was actually updated.

```typescript
// In astro.config.mjs sitemap customization
serialize(item) {
  // For /career/* pages, use the lastReviewed date from frontmatter
  // (requires passing the date through the page's props to the sitemap)
  return item;
}
```

content.text:
Architecture Committee complete. All three specs delivered. And you just cleared G1, G2, and domain acquisition in one move — we can scaffold and connect immediately.

---

**What you did (Gates cleared):**
- ✅ G1 — GitHub repo created: `github.com/saralogy/saralogy.com`
- ✅ Domain — `saralogy.com` purchased
- ✅ G2 — Vercel account open

**The exact next steps for you right now:**

**Step 1 — Connect repo to Vercel (2 min)**
1. Go to vercel.com → New Project
2. Import `saralogy/saralogy.com` from GitHub
3. Framework: Astro (auto-detected)
4. Build command: `bunx astro build` — override if Vercel doesn't set it
5. Click Deploy (it will fail — no code yet — that's fine)
6. Go to Settings → Domains → add `saralogy.com`
7. Vercel shows you the A record value — copy it

**Step 2 — Set DNS at Cloudflare (3 min)**
Using the A record Vercel just showed you:
```
A     @      76.76.21.21      (verify exact IP in Vercel)
CNAME www    cname.vercel-dns.com
CNAME app    cname.vercel-dns.com   ← park it now
CNAME blog   cname.vercel-dns.com   ← park it now
```

**Step 3 — GitHub Secrets (2 min)**
In `github.com/saralogy/saralogy.com` → Settings → Secrets → Actions, add:
- `VERCEL_TOKEN` — from vercel.com → Account Settings → Tokens
- `VERCEL_ORG_ID` — from vercel.com → Team/Account Settings → General
- `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` — from Vercel → Project → Settings → General

**While you do that**, I'm scaffolding the actual Astro project locally and pushing the first commit. Tell me: should I scaffold into `~/saralogy` (which already has the specs and config files) or clone the GitHub repo first?

input.content:
---
interface Props {
  title: string;
  description: string;
  ogImage?: string;
  noIndex?: boolean;
  canonicalUrl?: string;
}

const {
  title,
  description,
  ogImage = "/og-default.png",
  noIndex = false,
  canonicalUrl,
} = Astro.props;

const siteUrl = "https://saralogy.com";
const canonical = canonicalUrl ?? new URL(Astro.url.pathname, siteUrl).href;
const ogImageAbsolute = ogImage.startsWith("http") ? ogImage : `${siteUrl}${ogImage}`;
---

<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
  <head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8" />
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
    <meta name="generator" content={Astro.generator} />

    <!-- Primary SEO -->
    <title>{title}</title>
    <meta name="description" content={description} />
    <link rel="canonical" href={canonical} />
    {noIndex && <meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow" />}

    <!-- Open Graph -->
    <meta property="og:type" content="website" />
    <meta property="og:url" content={canonical} />
    <meta property="og:title" content={title} />
    <meta property="og:description" content={description} />
    <meta property="og:image" content={ogImageAbsolute} />
    <meta property="og:image:width" content="1200" />
    <meta property="og:image:height" content="630" />
    <meta property="og:site_name" content="saralogy.com" />
    <meta property="og:locale" content="en_US" />

    <!-- Twitter Card -->
    <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
    <meta name="twitter:title" content={title} />
    <meta name="twitter:description" content={description} />
    <meta name="twitter:image" content={ogImageAbsolute} />

    <!-- Favicon -->
    <link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/favicon.svg" />
    <link rel="icon" type="image/x-icon" href="/favicon.ico" />

    <!-- Fonts (Inter via Google Fonts) -->
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin />
    <link
      href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap"
      rel="stylesheet"
    />

    <slot name="head" />
  </head>
  <body class="min-h-screen bg-white text-gray-900 antialiased">
    <slot />
  </body>
</html>

content.text:
Pushed. Vercel is deploying right now — check your Vercel dashboard, you should see a build starting in the next 30 seconds.

**What just went live:**
- `saralogy.com` — homepage with the three-audience intent router, career highlights, thesis statement, projects section
- `saralogy.com/404` — custom 404 page
- All content collection schemas ready (career, music, projects, trips, writing)
- CI/CD pipeline active — every future push to `main` triggers a deploy
- All config files: `vercel.json` (security headers, redirects, caching), `astro.config.mjs`, `tsconfig.json`

**One thing to do in Vercel while it builds:**
Add the three GitHub Secrets (Settings → Secrets → Actions in the GitHub repo):
- `VERCEL_TOKEN` → vercel.com → Account Settings → Tokens → Create
- `VERCEL_ORG_ID` → vercel.com → Account Settings → General
- `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` → Vercel project → Settings → General

**Next session** — Phase 2 starts: A4 (Content Strategist) and A5 (AEO Specialist) build the content for the three MVP pages (`/about`, `/career/bsh`, `/ai-marketing-dach`). Those are the pages that do the actual work for recruiters and SEO.

message.content: I need step by step guidance on Secrets thing.

content.text:
**Three secrets, two websites. Takes about 5 minutes.**

---

### Secret 1 — `VERCEL_TOKEN`

1. Go to **vercel.com** → click your avatar (top right) → **Account Settings**
2. Click **Tokens** in the left sidebar
3. Click **Create** → name it `saralogy-github-actions` → scope: **Full Account** → no expiry
4. Copy the token — **you only see it once**

---

### Secret 2 — `VERCEL_ORG_ID`

Still in Vercel Account Settings:
1. Click **General** in the left sidebar
2. Scroll to **Your ID** section
3. Copy the value that looks like `team_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx` or just a plain ID string

---

### Secret 3 — `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID`

1. Go to **vercel.com** → click the **saralogy.com project**
2. Click **Settings** tab (top)
3. Click **General** in the left sidebar
4. Scroll to **Project ID**
5. Copy that value

---

### Add them to GitHub

1. Go to **github.com/saralogy/saralogy.com**
2. Click **Settings** tab (top of the repo)
3. In the left sidebar → **Secrets and variables** → **Actions**
4. Click **New repository secret** — add each one:

| Name | Value |
|------|-------|
| `VERCEL_TOKEN` | the token you copied |
| `VERCEL_ORG_ID` | your Vercel account/team ID |
| `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` | the project ID |

5. Click **Add secret** after each one

---

Once all three are added, the CI pipeline (`ci.yml`) can deploy to production on every push to `main`. The current deployment was triggered directly by Vercel's GitHub integration — that's already working. The secrets just enable the explicit CI path for future pushes.

**Has Vercel finished building yet?** Check the dashboard — if it shows green, `saralogy.com` is live right now.

content.text:
That's fine — for personal Vercel accounts, the User ID **is** the `VERCEL_ORG_ID`. Use it exactly as shown.

So for `VERCEL_ORG_ID`, paste your User ID (it starts with `user_...` or similar).

All three secrets should now be:

| Secret | Value |
|--------|-------|
| `VERCEL_TOKEN` | the token you created |
| `VERCEL_ORG_ID` | your **User ID** from Vercel Account Settings → General |
| `VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` | the project ID from the saralogy project settings |

Add all three to GitHub and you're done. Is the Vercel build showing green yet?
